## 2026-06-14: Tightened diagnostic workflow publish contracts

- Strengthened the Rust release-contract tests around default diagnostic workflows. Every `dotnet publish` block in CI/benchmark/memory/UI automation default workflows is now checked independently for `--self-contained false`, rust-only MSBuild props, and `WindowsAppSDKSelfContained=false`; a future second publish step can no longer be masked by another safe publish in the same file.
- Added `integration-tests.yml` to the no-runtime workflow scan and pinned its restore/build/test commands to `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` plus the same rust-only MSBuild props. It currently only restore/build/tests and must not add `dotnet publish`, retained worker features, or runtime extraction.
- No new dependency was needed; this stays in the existing text-contract test harness.

## 2026-06-14: Guarded retained WorkerCommand args before spawn

- Closed a retained-feature bypass found by the low-level runtime audit. `WorkerCommand::ensure_retained_worker_is_enabled(...)` now classifies both the executable path and its args before any process spawn or deferred `DOTNET_ROOT*` injection.
- A raw retained worker command such as `native-helper.exe --runtime=dotnet.exe`, `-- Easydict.Workers.LocalAi.exe`, `--script legacy-backend.ps1`, or `--target ...\hostfxr.dll` now requires explicit `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` before it can reach the I/O probe.
- No new dependency was needed. The fix reuses `lib/easydict-runtime-guards::command_target_is_retained_runtime_or_script_marker(...)`, which already owns the retained runtime/script command classifier.

Validation:

- `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check rs/crates/easydict_app/src/compat_client.rs rs/crates/easydict_app/tests/compat_client.rs`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --features retained-dotnet-workers --test compat_client raw_worker_command_to_retained_worker_requires_hybrid_runtime_profile_before_io_probe -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-14: Scanned Foundry Local CLI target bytes before spawn

- Closed the remaining content-level gap on the default rs app's only allowed external process boundary. `CommandFoundryLocalEndpointResolver` still accepts ordinary `foundry` PATH lookup, but whenever the CLI override resolves to an actual file it now reads the target bytes and rejects retained runtime/script markers before `Command::new(...)`.
- This blocks a renamed apphost or script payload called `foundry.exe` that still embeds strings such as `hostfxr.dll`, even when the path name itself looks native.
- No new dependency was needed. The implementation reuses `lib/easydict-runtime-guards::bytes_contain_retained_runtime_marker(...)`, matching the existing bundled-helper validation pattern in `lib/easydict-windows-shell`.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path lib/easydict-foundry-local/Cargo.toml --check`

## 2026-06-14: Guarded ShellExecute URL targets in the Windows shell lib

- Closed the `open_url(...)` ShellExecute boundary found by the Rust/lib audit. `lib/easydict-windows-shell` now accepts only `http://` and `https://` targets, preserves blank URL no-op behavior, and rejects retained runtime/script markers through `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` before calling `ShellExecuteW`.
- Added lib unit coverage for allowed HTTP/HTTPS URLs and rejected PowerShell/Pwsh, `.ps1`, `file://`, `workers/`, `dotnet/host/fxr`, and `hostfxr.dll` targets. Added default API boundary coverage that requires URL validation before platform delegation.

- Closed a native-messaging bridge destination gap found by the Rust/lib audit. `parse_browser_registrar_args --bridge-root-name` now rejects retained runtime/script component names through `lib/easydict-runtime-guards`, so a custom bridge root cannot be `dotnet`, `workers`, `Easydict.CompatHost*`, `Easydict.Workers.*`, PowerShell/Pwsh, `.ps1`, or `hostfxr.dll`.

## 2026-06-14: Tightened default CI runtime profiles and desktop registry command targets

- Closed several default CI/diagnostic runtime-profile gaps found by parallel read-only audits. UI automation WinUI unit tests, the performance benchmark workflow, PR memory gate, and nightly memory profile now explicitly set `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and pass `-p:RuntimeProfile=${{ env.RUNTIME_PROFILE }}` plus `-p:EnableInProcLongDocFallback=false` on WinUI restore/build/publish paths.
- Updated ARM64 MSIX smoke failure guidance so Rust-only/unknown profile users are pointed to the direct `easydict_packager pack-rs-portable` path, keeping `Package-Portable.ps1` as compatibility-only wording.
- Added a desktop integration registry guard: shell verb, protocol, and startup registration helpers now reject retained runtime, worker, or script command targets before writing HKCU command values. This reuses the existing `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` command-target classifier; no new library was needed.
- Added Rust contract coverage for benchmark and memory workflows, expanded the UI automation MSIX/profile contract to cover the parallel WinUI unit-test job, and added desktop integration unit coverage for rejected registry command targets.

## 2026-06-14: Rejected legacy CLI worker options by default and rechecked Collins MDD

- Tightened the default `easydict_cli` parser so `--host`, `--host-arg`, and `--app-dir` now return `UnknownOption` in the default build instead of being accepted as retained-worker no-ops. Compatibility parsing remains available only under the explicit `retained-dotnet-workers` feature.
- Added a default API boundary test that requires any legacy CLI option arms to be both feature-gated and paired with a default unknown-option guard.
- Kept the browser extension packaging shim on the Rust packager path with a fake-cargo contract for `browser-extension/scripts/Package-Extension.ps1 -> easydict_packager package-browser-extension`.
- Closed another MSIX validation gap through the parallel QDC/UI automation worker. QDC deploy/install now defaults `RuntimeProfile` to `rust-only`, copies or locates the Rust `easydict_msix_validate` binary, and validates the MSIX before cert import, uninstall, or `Add-AppxPackage`. The UI automation workflow now fixes both runtime profile env vars to `rust-only`, passes `-p:RuntimeProfile=${{ env.RUNTIME_PROFILE }}` to restore/publish, and validates the MSIX before upload and before install.
- Re-ran the Collins COBUILD English Usage corpus specified by the user, including the companion `.mdd`. The real corpus still reports `Encrypted=2`, 1517 MDX keywords, and one MDD resource `\cceu.css` with 4579 raw CSS bytes. Settings discovery, native HTML inlining, and Quick Translate all passed against the same files.
- No new parser dependency was needed. The in-tree `lib/rs-mdict` fork remains the right reuse point because it already owns the MDict encryption, key normalization, MDD resource lookup, and app-level data-URL inlining semantics.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --check`

## 2026-06-14: Aligned stale .NET packaging tests with hybrid-only ZIP helper gating

- Updated `dotnet/tests/Easydict.WinUI.Tests/Services/WorkerPackagingTests.cs` so the legacy .NET test oracle no longer treats `zip-directory` as evidence for the default rs portable release path. The test now scopes assertions to `publish-rs-portable` and `publish-msix`: rs portable must call direct `pack-rs-portable`, while the legacy/hybrid ZIP step must use `easydict_packager --features hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging -- zip-directory`.
- Tightened the legacy `dotnet/scripts/publish.ps1 -CreateZip` assertion so it requires the same explicit hybrid packager feature before `zip-directory`. This keeps the stale .NET-side contract in sync with the Rust release-contract tests added earlier.
- No new library was needed. This is test coverage alignment for the already-migrated Rust packager boundary.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior legacy_publish_create_zip_is_hybrid_named_and_excluded_from_rs_release_contract -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior rs_portable_release_path_forces_rust_only_runtime_profile -- --exact --nocapture`
- `dotnet test tests/Easydict.WinUI.Tests/Easydict.WinUI.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~WorkerPackagingTests" --no-restore` was retried after a 30s wait but still failed before running the filtered tests because unrelated long-document/layout/table test sources currently do not compile (`DocLayoutYoloService`, `LayoutDetectionStrategy`, `TableStructureRecognitionService`, `DocumentOutputMode`, and related stale symbols).

## 2026-06-14: Hid build-rust-helpers legacy alias knobs from the default API

- Tightened the helper build API after gating the legacy registrar alias itself. Default `BuildRustHelpersOptions` now only contains the Rust helper build inputs, and default `copy_built_rust_helpers(...)` only copies the four Rust helper executables. The `include_legacy_registrar_alias` field, `runtime_profile` field, alias-specific error variants, and extended copy arguments compile only with `hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging`.
- The default `build-rust-helpers` CLI no longer parses `--runtime-profile`; that knob is reserved for the explicit hybrid feature path where it must pair with `--include-legacy-registrar-alias`.
- No new library was needed. This is another Rust packager API-surface cleanup around already migrated helper binaries.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --check`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager default_build_rejects_runtime_profile_flag_before_workspace -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --features hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging build_rust_helpers_rejects_legacy_registrar_alias_without_explicit_hybrid_before_workspace -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-14: Moved standalone zip-directory behind the hybrid packager feature

- Tightened the packager CLI boundary: default `easydict_packager` usage no longer advertises `zip-directory`, and invoking that standalone command in a default build now follows the unknown-command path. The internal private `zip_directory(...)` implementation remains available because `pack-rs-portable` uses it to create the first rs portable ZIP before `validate-rs-portable`; the public standalone ZIP API is feature-gated for hybrid packaging.
- Updated the retained legacy/hybrid callers that still need the standalone ZIP helper. The release workflow's legacy ZIP step and `dotnet/scripts/publish.ps1 -CreateZip` now run `easydict_packager` with `--features hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging` before `zip-directory`.
- No new library was needed. This is a packager CLI/feature-boundary cleanup around the existing Rust ZIP implementation, keeping rs portable callers on `pack-rs-portable`.

## 2026-06-14: Gated the legacy registrar alias behind the hybrid packager feature

- Tightened the helper packaging boundary again: default `easydict_packager` builds now reject `--include-legacy-registrar-alias` before workspace/output side effects and no longer advertise that flag in default usage. The legacy `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` alias copy code only compiles under `hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging`.
- Kept the explicit coexistence path working. `dotnet/scripts/Build-RustHelpers.ps1` now adds `--features hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging` only when `-IncludeLegacyRegistrarAlias` is requested, and it still forces child `cargo`/helper builds to `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`.
- No new library was needed. This is a Rust packager feature boundary cleanup around the already migrated helper build path.

## 2026-06-14: Removed WinFluent Windows platform dev-dependency from the default app manifest

- Tightened the default app dependency boundary so `rs/crates/easydict_app/Cargo.toml` no longer lists `win_fluent_platform_win` even as a dev-dependency. This keeps the default rs app manifest aligned with the first portable release rule: platform/hybrid implementation crates stay outside the app crate unless explicitly needed by another package's own tests.
- Replaced the Quick Translate tray projection test that called `WindowsPlatformAdapter::plan_tray(...)` with an app-owned `TrayMenu` shape contract. The app test still verifies default item, submenu, separator, browser support, and command enablement structure, while native adapter projection remains the responsibility of the WinFluent platform crate.
- No new library was needed. This was a manifest/test-surface cleanup after the tray, shell, registry, and native task paths had already moved to Rust-owned helpers.

## 2026-06-13: Required Hybrid profile for the legacy browser registrar alias

- Tightened `easydict_packager build-rust-helpers` so `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` is no longer produced by the alias flag alone. Callers must pass `--runtime-profile hybrid --include-legacy-registrar-alias`; missing or explicit Rust-only profiles fail before workspace, output, or child cargo side effects.
- Updated `dotnet/scripts/Build-RustHelpers.ps1` to require `-RuntimeProfile Hybrid` when `-IncludeLegacyRegistrarAlias` is used, while still forcing the actual helper cargo/rustup child environment to `rust-only`.
- Updated legacy/hybrid release workflow, ARM64 smoke, Makefile, publish, and package-and-install callers to pass the explicit runtime profile when they request the alias. The first rs portable path remains alias-free.

## 2026-06-13: Namespaced rs DPAPI credential purpose

- Closed a settings/coexistence naming gap in the default rs credential path. New `edcred1:` credentials now use the rs-specific DPAPI optional entropy purpose `Easydict.Rs.LocalSettingsCredential.v1` instead of continuing to write the legacy WinUI purpose.
- Kept legacy settings import compatible: Rust still reads old WinUI `edcred1:` DPAPI payloads and marks them for normalization, so `load_settings_file(...)` rewrites them with the rs purpose alongside the existing plaintext/nested/`edloc1:` normalization path.
- No new dependency was needed. The implementation continues to use `lib/easydict-windows-credentials`, which wraps Windows DPAPI through the Microsoft `windows` crate.

## 2026-06-13: Namespaced rs protocol activation scheme

- Closed the remaining Windows global activation-name coexistence gap in the default rs app. The rs portable now registers `easydict-rs://` through the app-owned desktop integration path instead of overwriting the dotnet app's legacy `easydict://` protocol key.
- Kept startup activation compatibility: if a legacy `easydict://ocr-translate` URI is passed directly to the rs executable, the Rust parser still maps it to the same OCR hotkey path. New/default rs registration and tests use `easydict-rs://ocr-translate`.
- No new dependency was needed. The change reuses `easydict_app::desktop_integration` and `lib/easydict-windows-registry`.

## 2026-06-13: Narrowed default app bundled helper launch to browser registrar

- Tightened the default rs app shell boundary after the Native Messaging split. `easydict_app::desktop_shell` no longer exposes a generic "run bundled executable" task that accepts an arbitrary helper name; the app-level task is now browser-registrar-specific and always launches `easydict_browser_registrar.exe`.
- Kept the actual process boundary inside `lib/easydict-windows-shell`, which already validates helper names, rejects retained `.NET` / script helper names, checks the target file type, and scans helper bytes for retained runtime markers before `Command::new(...)`.
- No new dependency was needed. This is an app API surface narrowing over the existing Rust-owned shell wrapper.

## 2026-06-13: Namespaced rs browser Native Messaging host

- Closed another first-release portable coexistence gap. The rs browser registrar now defaults to the Native Messaging host name `com.easydict.rs.bridge` instead of legacy `com.easydict.bridge`, so installing browser support from the rs portable no longer overwrites the dotnet app's Chrome/Firefox Native Messaging registry key.
- Kept the staged bridge directory under the existing rs-specific `%LOCALAPPDATA%\EasydictRs\browser-bridge` root and kept legacy bridge-root rejection unchanged.
- Updated the browser extension scripts to try `com.easydict.rs.bridge` first and fall back to `com.easydict.bridge`, allowing updated extensions to work with the rs portable while still supporting an installed legacy/dotnet host.
- Synchronized the native bridge module constant with the rs host name. No new dependency was added; this is a naming/registration ownership split.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --check`
- `node --check browser-extension\background.js; node --check browser-extension\setup.js`

## 2026-06-13: Added real Collins MDD coverage through settings and Quick Translate

- Rechecked reusable MDX/MDD parser options before editing. Current Rust crates still include `rs-mdict`, `mdict-rs`, and `readmdict`, but the in-tree MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork already owns Easydict's MDX/MDD route, encryption boundaries, resource-key semantics, and app-level data-URL inlining. No parser dependency changed.
- Added an opt-in settings-storage regression using `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDX` / `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDD`: a legacy imported MDX entry with no saved `MddFilePaths` now proves it discovers the real Collins companion `.mdd`.
- Added an opt-in Quick Translate regression using the same real Collins files. The test imports the real MDX, confirms the companion MDD is discovered, runs the Rust-native `mdx::` request, verifies `\cceu.css` is inlined as `data:text/css;base64,...`, and confirms rich HTML is retained only through `rawHtml` while plain `translatedText` stays readable.
- Re-ran the full `lib/rs-mdict` real-corpus integration suite against `C:\Users\johnn\Downloads\collins-cobuild-english-usage\Collins COBUILD English Usage.mdx` plus the companion `.mdd`; all 24 tests passed. The corpus reports `Encrypted=2` key-info-only MDX/MDD, 1517 MDX keywords, one MDD resource `\cceu.css`, and 4579 raw CSS bytes.

## 2026-06-13: Split rs settings storage from legacy settings migration

- Closed a first-release portable coexistence gap in the settings path. Runtime settings now default to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\EasydictRs\settings.json` through `default_settings_storage_path()`, while `EASYDICT_SETTINGS_DIR` still overrides the storage directory for UI automation, CLI tests, and isolated smoke runs.
- Kept the legacy `.NET` settings directory as a migration source instead of a runtime storage target. `migrate_settings_file(Default::default())` now reads `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Easydict\settings.json` and writes the migrated copy to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\EasydictRs\settings.json`, so launching the rs portable can import old settings without rewriting the dotnet app's live settings file.
- No new dependency was needed. The slice only separates path ownership and uses the existing Rust settings migrator/storage code.
- Added path-contract coverage for the rs-specific default storage directory, the `EASYDICT_SETTINGS_DIR` override, and default legacy-to-rs migration copy behavior. The env-sensitive tests now serialize their process-environment overrides so full test-binary runs do not race on `LOCALAPPDATA` or `EASYDICT_SETTINGS_DIR`.

## 2026-06-13: Moved default rs cache roots under EasydictRs

- Followed the portable coexistence audit and split the remaining runtime cache/data fallback roots from the dotnet `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Easydict` directory. `app_data::default_user_data_directory()` now owns the rs default `%LOCALAPPDATA%\EasydictRs` root.
- OpenVINO/NLLB assets, layout model assets, CJK font downloads, settings runtime-status probes, and LongDoc translation cache now use the rs root whenever `SettingsSnapshot.cache_dir` is blank or absent. Explicit `cache_dir` settings still win, so user-configured portable/test cache roots remain respected.
- Local dictionary index is intentionally unchanged in this slice because it was already documented as a compatible shared cache with the dotnet app, not a runtime/process boundary.
- No new dependency was needed; this is a small path ownership helper rather than a platform-directory abstraction.
- Updated blank-cache/default-root coverage for layout models, fonts, OpenVINO status/download roots, and LongDoc translation cache.

## 2026-06-13: Namespaced rs shell and startup registry entries

- Split two Windows global registration names that would otherwise make first-release rs portable compete with an installed dotnet app.
- The rs OCR shell verb id is now `EasydictRsOCR` instead of legacy `EasydictOCR`, while keeping the same user-visible "OCR Translate" label and `--ocr-translate` activation argument.
- The rs startup Run value is now `EasydictRs` instead of legacy `Easydict`, so enabling launch-at-startup in the portable does not overwrite the dotnet startup command.
- At this point the `easydict://` protocol scheme and Native Messaging host id were intentionally left for separate compatibility/channel decisions; later 2026-06-13 slices moved Native Messaging to `com.easydict.rs.bridge` and protocol registration to `easydict-rs://`.

## 2026-06-13: Moved rs app icons out of the dotnet project tree

- Trimmed another default rs dependency on the legacy project layout. The app previously embedded service icon PNGs from `dotnet/src/Easydict.WinUI/Assets/ServiceIcons` at compile time; those assets are now owned by `rs/crates/easydict_app/resources/service-icons`.
- Moved `AppIcon.ico` into `rs/crates/easydict_app/resources` as well. Tray icon discovery now checks the portable/current directory and rs resources instead of walking up to `dotnet/src/Easydict.WinUI/AppIcon.ico`.
- `pack-rs-portable` now stages `AppIcon.ico` beside `Easydict.Rust.exe`, the Rust helper executables, and `README-portable.txt`, so first-release portable builds have the tray icon file without depending on a sibling dotnet checkout.
- Rechecked reusable resource-embedding crates first. `rust-embed` and `include_dir` are good fits for whole asset trees, but this path only needs a fixed set of image bytes plus one portable icon file, so the implementation stays on Rust's standard `include_bytes!` and explicit package staging without adding a proc-macro dependency.
- Added a default boundary regression so production rs sources cannot load app assets from `dotnet/src/Easydict.WinUI` again, and verified the copied PNG/ICO hashes match the original assets.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test default_api_boundary_behavior default_rs_app_embeds_service_icons_from_crate_owned_resources -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior default_tray_menu_covers_migration_contract -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager tests::stage_rust_portable_payload_copies_preview_helpers_and_readme -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager tests::validate_rs_portable_accepts_rust_only_directory_payload -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager tests::validate_rs_portable_rejects_missing_required_first_release_payload -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior pack_rs_portable_child_cargo_is_forced_to_rust_only_runtime_profile -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior pack_rs_portable_creates_and_validates_zip_without_retained_dotnet_payload -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --lib`
- SHA-256 comparison of all 19 copied service icon PNGs and `AppIcon.ico` against the existing dotnet assets.

## 2026-06-13: Kept the packaged Rust GUI alias production-only

- Closed a first-release portable entrypoint leak found during the parallel audit. `pack-rs-portable` currently copies `easydict_preview_iced.exe` to `Easydict.Rust.exe`; inherited `EASYDICT_PREVIEW_*` variables must not turn that public production entrypoint into the screenshot/preview harness.
- `preview_mode_requested()` now checks the current executable name. `Easydict.Rust.exe` ignores preview environment variables and stays on the production initial state, while the real `easydict_preview_iced.exe` keeps the UI automation preview behavior.
- Preview-only side effects in `PreviewApp::new()` are now guarded by the same mode decision, so schema dumps, auto-toggle, scroll injection, and pop-button preview state do not run through the packaged production alias.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_preview_iced preview_mode -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_preview_iced tests::production_initial_state_removes_preview_demo_content_and_loads_settings -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_preview_iced --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`

## 2026-06-13: Scanned bundled helpers before runtime launch

- Closed the next runtime execution gap from the parallel audit: packaging and browser registrar already scan helper bytes, but `lib/easydict-windows-shell` only checked bundled helper names, regular-file status, symlinks, and reparse points before `Command::new(...)`.
- `validate_bundled_executable_target(...)` now reads the target executable and reuses `easydict_runtime_guards::bytes_contain_retained_runtime_marker(...)`. A same-name helper polluted with retained `.NET`, PowerShell, TTS, or WinForms marker bytes now fails locally before spawn.
- Added a shell-lib regression with a fake `easydict_browser_registrar.exe` containing both ASCII `hostfxr.dll` and UTF-16LE `System.Windows.Forms`; the regular fake helper still passes.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-shell/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior browser_support_messages_run_bundled_registrar_commands -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Made standalone rs portable marker validation usable

- Revisited the retained-runtime marker scanner after the real portable smoke. `pack-rs-portable` validated the staged payload, but invoking the debug `validate-rs-portable` CLI directly on the same 79 MB directory took minutes, which is too sluggish for a release gate.
- Rechecked reuse options before editing. The Rust `aho-corasick` crate is already present elsewhere in the workspace lock and provides multi-pattern search with ASCII case-insensitive matching, which fits the retained marker scanner better than hand-rolling repeated `windows()` scans.
- Added `aho-corasick` as a direct dependency of `lib/easydict-runtime-guards`. The public `bytes_contain_retained_runtime_marker(...)` API is unchanged, but it now builds one cached Aho-Corasick matcher containing both ASCII and UTF-16LE forms from the existing encoded marker table.
- Verified the real staged portable directory again with the standalone debug packager: `validate-rs-portable --package rs/target/portable-smoke/easydict-rs-portable-win-x64` completed successfully in about 3.2 seconds and checked all 6 entries. The manual retained-marker scan over the five shipped executables still reports OK.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-runtime-guards/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager validate_rs_portable_rejects_allowlisted_exe_that_contains -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager validate_rs_portable -- --nocapture`
- `C:\Users\johnn\Documents\work\easydict_win32.refactor\rs\target\debug\easydict_packager.exe validate-rs-portable --package C:\Users\johnn\Documents\work\easydict_win32.refactor\rs\target\portable-smoke\easydict-rs-portable-win-x64`
- Manual marker scan of `Easydict.Rust.exe`, `easydict-native-bridge.exe`, `easydict_browser_registrar.exe`, `easydict_cli.exe`, and `easydict_long_doc.exe` for ASCII/UTF-16LE retained .NET, PowerShell, System.Speech, and System.Windows.Forms markers.

## 2026-06-13: Gated WinFluent legacy backends and verified rs portable payload

- Followed the parallel audits on packaging feature propagation and runtime marker coverage. No new dependency was added; the change reuses the existing app-owned Rust file-dialog/TTS helpers and the shared `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` validator.
- Put WinFluent's old PowerShell file/folder dialogs behind `legacy-powershell-dialogs` and the old PowerShell/System.Speech TTS backend behind `legacy-powershell-tts`. Both features default off; `easydict_preview_iced` now depends on `win_fluent_backend_iced` with `default-features = false`.
- Encoded retained runtime content/path/component marker tables inside `lib/easydict-runtime-guards`, including `System.Windows.Forms`, so apps linking the scanner no longer self-report `hostfxr.dll`, `.runtimeconfig.json`, or `Easydict.CompatHost*` strings from the scanner itself.
- Confirmed the real first-release portable path builds and validates as Rust-only: `pack-rs-portable --configuration Release --no-zip` produced 6 entries under `rs/target/portable-smoke/easydict-rs-portable-win-x64`, 79.03 MB total, with no retained .NET/PowerShell/WinForms marker hits in the five shipped executables.
- Re-ran the Collins COBUILD real MDX/MDD tests with the correct `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDX` / `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDD` variables. The parser saw the encrypted MDX/MDD pair and the app route inlined `\cceu.css` as a data URI.

## 2026-06-13: Moved OCR named-event receiving to a Rust-owned stream

- Followed the parallel desktop-boundary audit: the NativeBridge binary already signals `Local\EasydictRs-OcrTranslate` through `lib/easydict-windows-ipc`, but the app still received that event through WinFluent's named-event subscription/runtime adapter.
- Added `NamedEventListener` to `lib/easydict-windows-ipc`, wrapping create/wait/auto-reset named-event behavior next to the existing signal helper.
- Added `easydict_app::named_event`, an app-owned `Task::stream(...)` source that listens for the rs OCR event and emits the same OCR hotkey message. `EasydictApp::subscription()` no longer declares `Subscription::named_event`, and `RuntimePlan` no longer carries named-event desktop integration entries.
- Kept tray, hotkey, shell verb, and protocol surfaces unchanged in this slice. Shell/protocol registration still uses the existing Rust-owned `desktop_integration` tasks.
- Updated the stale `.NET` WorkerPackaging test oracle for ARM64 smoke so it expects a blank default `runtime_profile` instead of implicit `hybrid`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior shell_and_protocol_entries_cover_ocr_activation_contract -- --nocapture`

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_preview_iced preview_runtime_plan_captures_inner_desktop_integration_entries -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test native_bridge_behavior native_bridge_binary_signals_ocr_translate_event_through_real_binary -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior arm64_msix_smoke_requires_explicit_hybrid_runtime_profile -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Reconfirmed Silent OCR clipboard boundary

- Audited the Silent OCR completion path after a search hit on `PlatformCommand::WriteClipboardText`. The hit was a negative regression assertion, not production code: `OcrResultAction::CopyText` now flows through `clipboard_write_task(...)`, which calls `easydict_app::clipboard::write_clipboard_text(...)` on a Rust-owned future.
- No new dependency was added. This keeps the earlier clipboard reuse decision: explicit copy/read paths and monitoring reuse the existing `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` Win32 helper instead of adding a second clipboard backend such as `clipboard-win` or `arboard`.
- Renamed the Silent OCR regression to `silent_ocr_outcome_uses_rust_clipboard_task` so future `WriteClipboardText` searches read as a retired-command boundary check.
- Narrowed the clipboard-monitor and mouse-selection stop assertions to the actual background-stream contract. `SaveSettingsChanges` may also schedule a startup-registration future, so the tests now require no monitor/hook stream and verify the Rust singleton has stopped instead of requiring the entire save task to be `none`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test ocr_behavior silent_ocr_outcome_uses_rust_clipboard_task -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior clipboard -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior saving_mouse_selection_setting_starts_and_stops_rust_owned_hook_stream -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check rs/crates/easydict_app/tests/ocr_behavior.rs`
- `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check rs/crates/easydict_app/tests/quick_translate_behavior.rs`

- Added `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_CLIPBOARD_MONITOR_SMOKE=1` coverage in `easydict_app::clipboard`: the test starts the real app-owned monitor stream, writes external clipboard text through the Windows helper, verifies the stream emits it, then writes via `write_clipboard_text(...)` and verifies that app self-written text is suppressed once.

## 2026-06-13: Added a real VS Code text insertion smoke

- Continued the core input/replace path without changing production dependencies. No new library was needed; the smoke reuses the existing `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` Win32 clipboard/focus/SendInput boundary.
- Added `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TEXT_INSERTION_VSCODE_SMOKE=1` coverage that opens a unique temporary file in a new VS Code window, focuses the Monaco editor, captures the foreground insertion target, inserts text through `insert_text_into_target(...)`, then overwrites the clipboard with a sentinel and uses Ctrl+A/C to prove the editor itself contains the inserted text.

## 2026-06-13: Added a real terminal no-Ctrl+C TextSelection smoke

- Closed the other core TextSelection evidence gap with an env-gated Windows smoke. Production dependencies did not change; `easydict_app` only gained a Windows `dev-dependency` on the already-used Microsoft `windows` crate for test-only console/window APIs.
- Added `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TERMINAL_TEXT_SELECTION_SMOKE=1` coverage in `text_selection_behavior`: the parent test copies the current test binary to a temporary `pwsh.exe`, launches it in a new console, focuses that console HWND, and calls the real `capture_native_selected_text()` path.
- The helper child installs a console Ctrl handler and stays alive until a stop marker is written. The parent asserts capture returns no text, the helper remains alive, and no Ctrl+C/SIGINT marker is written, proving the real foreground HWND/PID/process-name route stays UIA-only for terminal-like targets instead of entering clipboard fallback.
- This is intentionally narrower than a full Windows Terminal/PowerShell E2E; it is the stable regression guard for the dangerous behavior: sending Ctrl+C into a terminal.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test text_selection_behavior terminal_text_selection_does_not_send_ctrl_c_to_console_when_enabled -- --exact --nocapture`
- `$env:EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TERMINAL_TEXT_SELECTION_SMOKE='1'; cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test text_selection_behavior terminal_text_selection_does_not_send_ctrl_c_to_console_when_enabled -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Added a real Notepad UIA provider smoke for TextSelection

- Continued the core selected-text migration without adding a dependency. The Windows selection backend stays in `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` on Microsoft's official `windows` crate; this slice adds real-provider evidence rather than another wrapper.
- Added an `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_UIA_TEXT_SELECTION_SMOKE=1` gated smoke that opens a unique temporary text file in Notepad, finds that document window by title instead of process id to handle modern single-instance Notepad, selects all text, and verifies `selected_text_via_uia_with_timeout(...)` reads the selection through UIA TextPattern.
- The smoke closes only the unique document window and removes the temporary file. Default test runs keep it skipped, so regular CI does not launch Notepad.

## 2026-06-13: Added a real Windows screen-capture smoke

- Rechecked screenshot library options before changing this slice. `windows-capture` / `scap` are better fits for continuous Windows Graphics Capture/video pipelines, while `screenshots` is a broader cross-platform wrapper. The OCR capture path only needs one-shot BGRA region capture and window enumeration, so the existing lib-owned Microsoft `windows` crate wrapper remains the smallest and most controllable route.
- Added an opt-in real desktop smoke to `lib/easydict-windows-screen-capture`. With `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_SCREEN_CAPTURE_SMOKE=1`, it captures a 1x1 BGRA pixel through the same `BitBlt` / `GetDIBits` path, verifies the 4-byte pixel file and screen rect, then enumerates visible top-level windows through the same `EnumWindows` path.
- The smoke skips by default when the env var is unset, so normal CI/developer tests do not require an interactive desktop.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-screen-capture/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Verified Collins COBUILD real MDX/MDD corpus

- Used the local Collins COBUILD English Usage MDX/MDD files through `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDX` / `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDD`, without hard-coding the user-machine path into the repository or adding a dependency.
- Confirmed the real `.mdd` opens through `lib/rs-mdict` as `Encrypted=2` / key-info-only and exposes `\cceu.css`; raw lookup returns the CSS bytes and MIME inference reports `text/css`.
- Added an app-level env-gated real-corpus test that runs `run_native_mdx_lookup` against the real MDX and companion MDD. The `ability` entry's `<link href="cceu.css">` is rewritten to `data:text/css;base64,...`, proving the product route, not only the parser, handles this real dictionary pair.
- Updated the `lib/rs-mdict` real-corpus integration tests so lookup/contains/fetch/fuzzy assertions use env-configurable corpus queries. The defaults now match this Collins Usage dictionary (`ability`, `about`, `apply`, `aply -> apply`) instead of assuming demo entries like `hello`, `world`, and `apple`.
- Tightened the MDX MDD rescan path so newly discovered same-stem/numbered companions are appended without dropping already saved extra MDD paths.

## 2026-06-13: Hid retained worker dedupe behind explicit hybrid profile

- Continued the packaging/tooling boundary cleanup after a read-only subagent audit. No new dependency was added; the existing Rust `easydict_msix_validate` dedupe logic remains the implementation.
- `easydict_msix_validate dedupe-worker-shared` is no longer listed in default usage, and the command now fails before reading the publish directory unless the caller passes `--runtime-profile hybrid`.
- `dotnet/scripts/Dedupe-WorkerSharedFiles.ps1` now requires `-RuntimeProfile Hybrid`, rejects omitted/rust-only profiles locally, and forwards `--runtime-profile hybrid` to the Rust CLI. Release workflow, ARM64 smoke, `dotnet/Makefile`, and `package-and-install.ps1` now pass that explicit profile from their already hybrid-gated paths.
- `rs/README.md` moved the dedupe example under a hybrid/coexistence-only note, so default MSIX validator smoke checks do not advertise retained worker maintenance.

- Root README/README_ZH now show `cargo run --manifest-path rs\Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager -- pack-rs-portable --workspace rs ...` as the default portable build command, followed only by a compatibility note for the PowerShell shim.
- `.github/workflows/release-publish.yml` now builds rs portable ZIPs with `cargo run --manifest-path Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager -- pack-rs-portable --workspace . ...` from the Rust workspace instead of invoking `./scripts/Package-Portable.ps1`.

## 2026-06-13: Re-verified Rust-native MDD resource support

- Confirmed the MDD path is core-complete for the first rs portable target without adding another dependency: the in-tree `lib/rs-mdict` reader handles plain and zlib MDD resources, record-encrypted resources through a caller-provided key-header transform, header-aware resource-key lookup, cross-record raw bytes, and resource MIME metadata.
- Confirmed the product route supplies that transform from the app-side MDX dictionary snapshot when credentials are present, then uses the same Rust-native MDD readers for companion discovery, first-hit resource lookup, HTML `data:` URL inlining, and Quick Translate `rawHtml` gating.
- `Mdd::new()` remains a low-level parser constructor; encrypted companion resources in the app route through `RsMdictMddReaderFactory`, so the credential algorithm stays in `easydict_app::mdx_native` next to MDX credential derivation.

## 2026-06-13: Blocked cmd.exe shell trampolines in runtime command guards

- Continued the default rs external-process boundary hardening after a read-only subagent audit. No new dependency was added; this stays in the shared `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` classifier used by Foundry Local CLI overrides and bundled helper launch guards.
- `command_target_is_retained_runtime_or_script_marker(...)` now parses the command head before path normalization, so `cmd /c dotnet.exe`, `cmd.exe /c powershell.exe`, and quoted `C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe ...` are rejected before any spawn attempt. Legitimate native shims such as `foundry.cmd` remain allowed.
- Added CLI coverage proving `EASYDICT_FOUNDRY_LOCAL_CLI="cmd /c dotnet.exe"` fails locally in the default `windows-local-ai` CLI route without falling through to retained worker/CompatHost probing.

## 2026-06-13: Matched TTS restart semantics with SAPI purge

- Rechecked the native API before adding another dependency. Microsoft SAPI already documents async `ISpVoice::Speak` plus `SPF_PURGEBEFORESPEAK` for interrupting current output, so the existing `windows` crate wrapper remains the best fit.
- `lib/easydict-windows-tts` now assigns each speech request a generation. Starting a newer request marks older playback sessions stale; older worker threads poll the SAPI completion event and purge their own voice with `SPF_PURGEBEFORESPEAK` before exiting.
- This keeps the app-facing TTS task fire-and-forget, while moving closer to the .NET service behavior where `SpeakAsync` stops any current playback before starting the next item.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-tts/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-tts/Cargo.toml --check`
- `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check lib/easydict-windows-tts/src/lib.rs rs/crates/easydict_app/src/tts.rs`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app tts --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior speak -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`

## 2026-06-13: Wired Rust-native TTS speed into SAPI playback

- Rechecked the TTS backend choice before changing code. No new third-party dependency is needed: the existing `lib/easydict-windows-tts` wrapper can use Microsoft's official `windows` crate and SAPI `ISpVoice::SetRate`, while avoiding the old WinFluent PowerShell / `System.Speech` path.
- `easydict_app::tts` now parses the active settings `TtsSpeed` value with the same effective bounds as the .NET service (`0.5..3.0`), and both result Speak actions and `AutoPlayTranslation` pass that speed into the Rust-owned TTS task.
- `lib/easydict-windows-tts` maps the clamped speaking-rate multiplier to SAPI's `-10..10` rate adjustment before scheduling async playback, so speed, language voice selection, startup error reporting, and background playback are all handled in the Rust-native library boundary.

## 2026-06-13: Hardened Rust-native Windows TTS startup reporting

- Rechecked reusable Rust TTS options before changing code. The broad `tts` crate remains more abstraction than the first rs portable needs, while `sapi-lite` wraps only part of SAPI; the existing in-tree wrapper under `lib/easydict-windows-tts` stays on Microsoft's official `windows` crate for direct SAPI access.
- The Windows TTS helper now starts SAPI playback on a background thread but waits for a startup handshake before returning. COM initialization, SAPI voice setup, language-specific voice enumeration, and `ISpVoice::Speak` scheduling failures are no longer swallowed by the helper thread.
- Switched the SAPI call to async speak and keep the voice alive on the worker thread with `WaitUntilDone`, so app-side Speak actions still do not wait for the full audio playback.
- Added an opt-in `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TTS_SMOKE=1` real SAPI smoke test for manual Windows verification; regular test runs skip it.

Validation:

## 2026-06-13: Added a real Win32 text selection/insertion smoke and fixed clipboard unlock semantics

- No new dependency was added. The Windows backend continues to use the official `windows` crate inside `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection`.
- Added an `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TEXT_SELECTION_SMOKE=1` gated smoke test that creates a temporary Win32 `EDIT` control, selects text, exercises the real foreground + `Ctrl+C` clipboard fallback, then captures the foreground target and inserts replacement text via the real clipboard + `Ctrl+V` path. The smoke restores the user's clipboard afterward.
- The smoke exposed a real Win32 edge case in our clipboard helper: `GlobalUnlock` returns zero when the lock count reaches zero and only indicates failure if `GetLastError()` is non-zero. The wrapper now handles that documented NO_ERROR success case for both clipboard reads and writes.
- The same pass made foreground activation more robust by attaching to the current foreground thread before `SetForegroundWindow`, while preserving external app child focus and only restoring direct focus for same-thread helper/smoke windows.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-text-selection/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture`
- `$env:EASYDICT_WINDOWS_TEXT_SELECTION_SMOKE='1'; cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-text-selection/Cargo.toml real_edit_control_smoke -- --nocapture`

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app text_insertion --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`

## 2026-06-13: Wired Rust Mouse Selection into the default app runtime

- No new dependency was needed. The existing `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` low-level hook wrapper now feeds an app-owned Rust stream, keeping unsafe Win32 hook code under `lib/`.
- `EasydictUiState` now owns the `MouseSelectionProducer`, so low-level mouse/keyboard events enter the same Rust state machine as the prior pure tests. The default app starts/stops the stream from the persisted `MouseSelectionTranslate` setting on startup and save/discard, and ignores residual hook events when the setting is off.
- Pending double/triple-click actions now create a real delayed completion task instead of only exposing timer metadata; completion re-enters the producer and maps to the existing native selected-text capture + PopButton chain. PopButton self-click suppression and excluded-app checks are applied at the app boundary.

## 2026-06-13: Hardened release contracts against negative rust-only workflow gates

- No new dependency was needed. This strengthens the existing Rust packager release-contract tests.
- Added a shared assertion that rejects workflow lines combining `RUNTIME_PROFILE`, `rust-only` / `rustonly`, and negative comparisons such as `!=` or `-ne`. Retained worker/runtime workflow steps must stay positively gated by explicit hybrid enablement instead of “anything not rust-only”.
- Applied the helper to the default release workflow and the hybrid MSIX workflow checks, so future rewrites like `${{ env.RUNTIME_PROFILE != 'rust-only' }}` or PowerShell `-ne 'rust-only'` do not bypass the current exact-string guard.

## 2026-06-13: Stopped Makefile from forwarding empty runtime profiles to legacy shims

- No new dependency was needed. This continues the legacy packaging runtime-profile hardening inside `dotnet/Makefile` and the existing Rust packager release-contract test.
- `msix-*`, `installer-*`, and MSIX runtime extraction paths now pass the normalized shell `$$runtime_profile` value into `Package-Msix.ps1`, `Build-Installer.ps1`, and `Extract-DotnetRuntime.ps1` instead of forwarding the raw Make variable `$(RUNTIME_PROFILE)`.
- The MSIX and installer targets also re-check explicit `hybrid` immediately before invoking their legacy PowerShell shims, so a split or reordered target cannot pass an empty profile down to a retained-runtime-producing path.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior legacy_dotnet_packaging_paths_reject_rust_only_and_require_hybrid_profile -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Guarded MSIX bundle validation with explicit hybrid profile

- No new dependency was needed. This is a release-orchestration guard in the existing GitHub Actions workflow and packager contract tests.
- Added an explicit `create-bundle` runtime-profile guard before bundle validation. The job now rejects empty, rust-only, and unknown profile values locally, and normalizes the environment to `RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` before passing it to `easydict_msix_validate verify-bundle-minversion`.
- This closes the remaining direct bundle path where `--runtime-profile "${{ env.RUNTIME_PROFILE }}"` could receive an empty workflow input if the DAG or job invocation changes later.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior legacy_dotnet_packaging_paths_reject_rust_only_and_require_hybrid_profile -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Made Rust-only MSIX validation reject the legacy registrar alias

- No new dependency was needed. This continues the helper packaging policy cleanup inside the existing Rust `easydict_msix_validate` payload layout validator.
- Split MSIX helper requirements by runtime profile: Rust-only/default validation now requires only the four Rust helper executables and rejects `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe`, while explicit hybrid/coexistence validation still requires the legacy alias for the current WinUI browser-support path.
- Tightened `prepare-package-inputs` so the Rust-only/default preflight also rejects a root `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` publish-dir payload before writing the prepared manifest; hybrid/coexistence prepare still leaves that alias for full validation.
- Updated MSIX and nested bundle fixtures so `rust_helper_entries()` represents the alias-free Rust-only payload and `hybrid_helper_entries()` represents the legacy/hybrid payload.
- Updated the migration checklist to keep the MSIX validator aligned with `build-rust-helpers`: the legacy alias is no longer a default package requirement.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_msix_validate --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_msix_validate --all-targets`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_msix_validate --check`

## 2026-06-13: Made the legacy browser registrar alias opt-in for helper builds

- No new dependency was needed. This is a packaging policy cleanup inside the existing Rust `easydict_packager` helper build path and the PowerShell shim that delegates to it.
- `easydict_packager build-rust-helpers` now defaults to copying only Rust helper executables. The legacy `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` compatibility alias is created only when callers pass `--include-legacy-registrar-alias`.
- `dotnet/scripts/Build-RustHelpers.ps1` mirrors that rule with `-IncludeLegacyRegistrarAlias`; legacy/hybrid WinUI portable and MSIX callers pass the switch explicitly, while first-release rs portable packaging remains alias-free.
- Updated the migration checklist so `build-rust-helpers` no longer reads as a generic alias-producing path. The alias is now documented as a legacy/hybrid coexistence requirement, not part of the default rs portable payload.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager copy_built_rust_helpers -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior build_rust_helpers_child_cargo_is_forced_to_rust_only_runtime_profile -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Centralized Foundry Local CLI runtime guard

- No new third-party dependency was needed. The default rs Foundry Local route already uses the native `foundry` CLI boundary, so this slice reuses the in-tree `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` policy instead of adding another parser or process-wrapper crate.
- Moved Foundry Local's `EASYDICT_FOUNDRY_LOCAL_CLI` retained runtime/script command classifier onto `easydict_runtime_guards::command_target_is_retained_runtime_or_script_marker(...)`. The shared guard now covers direct command names, canonical targets, `dotnet/` and `workers/` path components, `host/fxr` and shared framework layouts, loose runtime assemblies, retired Easydict helper/worker names, and `.ps1` script targets.
- Updated the default app source-boundary test so the only allowed external process boundary still belongs to `lib/easydict-foundry-local`, while retained runtime/script classification must be delegated to `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` instead of re-inlined in the Foundry wrapper.
- Added a local dictionary app-dir regression proving `run_local_dictionary_suggestion_request_with_app_dir(...)` ignores stale retained payloads under the supplied app dir and fails locally through the Rust-native MDX/index route.

Validation:

## 2026-06-13: Decoded HTML-escaped MDD resource references in Rust

- Rechecked the MDD request with a read-only subagent against the `.NET` service, WebView resource interception path, existing Rust fixtures, and migration docs. The active route remains the in-tree MIT `lib/rs-mdict` plus app-owned HTML rewrite/credential glue; no new parser dependency was added.
- Tightened the Rust MDD HTML inliner so resource references are decoded like browser/WebView input before lookup. This now covers `&amp;` query separators, numeric entities in Unicode paths, and `&quot;...&quot;`-wrapped CSS `url(...)` references before percent decoding and MDD key normalization.
- Added focused coverage for entity-escaped image, audio, `srcset`, and CSS resources while preserving external URLs and the existing first-hit / failure-skip MDD behavior.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml --lib mdd -- --nocapture`

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test settings_storage_behavior mdd -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Centralized retained runtime marker scanning for packages

- Reused `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` from `easydict_msix_validate` for both retained payload path classification and retained byte marker scanning. The MSIX validator no longer keeps its own marker table, path classifier, or ASCII / UTF-16LE scanning helpers.
- Moved the first-release rs portable retained payload path classifier onto the same library-owned policy, so rs portable ZIP validation and MSIX / nested bundle validation reject the same stale `.NET` runtime layouts, worker roots, loose assemblies, and retired helper payload names.
- Added source-boundary regressions proving both `easydict_msix_validate` and `easydict_packager` delegate retained marker checks to `easydict_runtime_guards` instead of re-inlining package-specific copies.

## 2026-06-13: Renamed legacy hybrid ZIP artifacts away from rs portable

- A parallel worker updated the release workflow so explicit hybrid/coexistence ZIPs are named `Easydict-legacy-hybrid-win-<platform>-<version>.zip` and uploaded as `easydict-legacy-hybrid-zip-*` artifacts instead of the old `easydict_win32-*` style name.
- The create-bundle job now downloads the clearly named legacy/hybrid ZIP artifacts, while the first rs release path remains isolated to `easydict-rs-portable-*`. This reduces the chance that retained `.NET` coexistence ZIPs are mistaken for the default rs portable package.
- The release contract test now guards the destination, upload artifact name, download pattern, and absence of the old ambiguous ZIP prefix on the hybrid workflow path.

## 2026-06-13: Rejected retired MSIX helper payloads by prefix

- Tightened the Rust MSIX payload validator so retired helper/tool names are rejected by file-name prefix rather than only by exact `.exe` names. This now covers `.dll`, `.deps.json`, `.runtimeconfig.json`, and similar stale `.NET` payload forms for old `Easydict.NativeBridge*`, `Easydict.BrowserRegistrar*`, `MSIXValidate*`, `EncryptSecret*`, `PdfToImages*`, `Easydict.CompatHost*`, and retired OCR worker entries.
- No new third-party library was needed; this is package-layout policy inside the existing Rust validator. The rule applies before the rust-only/hybrid runtime-profile branch, so both first rs portable/MSIX and explicit coexistence packages reject retired helper/tool residue.

## 2026-06-13: Scanned browser native bridge binaries before registration

- Added a second guard to the Rust browser Native Messaging registrar. In addition to rejecting legacy names and retained payload roots, `BrowserRegistrarCore::install(...)` now scans the source bridge bytes through `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` before copying and scans the staged bridge again before writing Chrome/Firefox manifests.
- `status` also treats a registration as installed only when the managed `easydict-native-bridge.exe` still passes the same retained payload/script marker scan. This closes the renamed-apphost case where a stale `.NET` bridge could have the expected Rust helper file name.
- No new dependency was added; this reuses the existing lib-owned ASCII / UTF-16LE marker scanner so packaging and browser registration share the same runtime residue policy.

## 2026-06-13: Hardened bundled shell helper launch names

- Tightened the Rust-owned desktop shell boundary after reusing the existing runtime-guard crate rather than adding a new dependency. Bundled helper names now keep the plain-file-name requirement and additionally reject retained runtime/script/helper markers before `std::process::Command` is constructed.
- `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` now treats `dotnet.exe` / `dotnet.cmd`, PowerShell / Pwsh executable variants, and existing retained worker/CompatHost/NativeBridge names as retained runtime components. `lib/easydict-windows-shell` reuses that classifier for `run_bundled_executable(...)`, while continuing to allow Rust-native helpers such as `easydict_browser_registrar.exe`.
- This protects the browser registrar install/uninstall path from being repointed at stale `.NET` runtime, PowerShell, or retained worker names in the first rs portable package.

## 2026-06-13: Narrowed OCR screen-capture DTOs to the Rust-owned helper

- No third-party crate was added. This continues using the existing Microsoft `windows` crate based helper under `lib/`; WinFluent remains only as the neutral `Task::perform(...)` wrapper used to schedule app messages.
- `screen_capture_request_from_selection(...)`, capture overlay background freeze, window snapshot requests, and OCR capture result conversion now all use the lib-owned DTOs. Source-boundary tests guard against reintroducing WinFluent capture DTOs into the native facade or OCR conversion path.

## 2026-06-13: Revalidated Rust-native MDD support and tightened encrypted route gating

- Rechecked current Rust MDict/MDD library options before changing code. The in-tree MIT `lib/rs-mdict` route remains the lowest-risk choice for the current app because it already shares MDX/MDD block parsing, supports raw MDD resource lookup, and avoids replacing the app's credential and HTML-rewrite integration with a newer parser surface.
- Confirmed the Rust-native MDD path covers companion `.mdd` discovery, first-hit lookup across multiple MDD files, raw bytes and MIME metadata, zlib/non-ASCII resource blocks, record-encrypted MDD files using the MDX dictionary credentials, and WebView-ready `data:` URL inlining for image/audio/CSS/resource references.
- Fixed the record-block encrypted MDX/MDD route gate so missing credentials are not reported as natively routable. A record-encrypted dictionary now only routes through the Rust reader when credentials are present and the regcode passes local validation.

- Continued hardening the first rs portable GUI entrypoint. `easydict_preview_iced.exe` is currently the binary copied to `Easydict.Rust.exe`, so its `PreviewApp` wrapper must expose the same runtime surfaces as the wrapped `EasydictApp`.
- No third-party crate is involved; this is an app/runtime wiring fix. `PreviewApp` now forwards `window_options`, `subscription`, `tray_menu`, `named_events`, `shell_verbs`, and `protocol_registrations` to the inner `EasydictApp`.
- This makes the packaged GUI runtime see the app-owned hotkeys, tray menu, named OCR event, mini/fixed/settings/capture/pop-button window options, shell context menu declarations, and `easydict://` protocol declaration instead of acting as a view/update-only preview wrapper.

## 2026-06-13: Ensured protocol registration through the Rust registry facade at app startup

- Continued the desktop-integration migration by making `Application::new(...)` enqueue a Rust-owned best-effort protocol registration task for the default `easydict://` activation scheme.
- No new third-party crate was needed for this slice. Protocol registration uses the existing `easydict_app::desktop_integration` facade and `lib/easydict-windows-registry`, preserving the same HKCU `Software\Classes\easydict` layout, `URL Protocol` marker, and `"exe" "%1"` command-line contract that the WinFluent platform adapter previously modeled.
- The startup path now calls `desktop_integration::register_protocol(...)` from a `Task::perform(...)` future and no longer needs WinFluent `Task::RegisterProtocol` for the app-owned protocol ensure. The `RuntimePlan` protocol declaration is still retained as declarative metadata for parity tests and future runtime integration.
- Kept the operation best-effort and `Message::Noop`-returning, matching the existing shell context-menu registration style and avoiding user-facing startup failures if HKCU protocol writes fail.

## 2026-06-13: Moved URL opening and bundled helper launch off WinFluent platform commands

- Continued the default rs desktop-integration cleanup by moving Settings/About external links, the tray Chrome extension link, and browser registrar install/uninstall launches away from WinFluent `PlatformCommand::OpenUrl` / `RunBundledExecutable`.
- Rechecked reusable crates before changing code. `open`, `webbrowser`, and `win-open` are viable general-purpose URL/default-app launch crates, but this slice only needs a narrow Windows shell boundary and should avoid cmd/script-style launch helpers. Added `lib/easydict-windows-shell` around Microsoft's official `windows` crate and native `ShellExecuteW`, plus a guarded bundled-executable launcher.
- Added `easydict_app::desktop_shell` as the app-owned facade. Browser support install/uninstall still launches `easydict_browser_registrar.exe` next to the current executable with the existing `install|uninstall --bridge-root-name EasydictRs` and optional `--chrome` / `--firefox` arguments, then keeps the 600ms browser-support status refresh. External links still resolve through the OS default handler and return `Message::Noop`.
- Hardened the bundled helper boundary so callers can only request a plain file name; absolute paths, subdirectories, `.` and `..` are rejected before process launch.

## 2026-06-13: Moved shell context-menu registration to the Rust registry helper

- Continued the desktop-integration migration by moving the Settings shell context-menu toggle away from WinFluent platform commands. The app now emits Rust-owned futures that call `easydict_app::desktop_integration` instead of `Task::RegisterShellVerb` / `Task::UnregisterShellVerb`.
- Rechecked reusable registry crates before changing code. `winreg` and Microsoft's `windows-registry` are both viable, but this slice extends the existing narrow `lib/easydict-windows-registry` helper so Browser registrar and shell/protocol registration share one HKCU registry boundary and keep exact unnamed/named `REG_SZ`, missing-key, and tree-delete semantics.
- Added `easydict_app::desktop_integration` as an app-owned facade for shell verb and protocol registration plans. The shell toggle writes the same `Software\Classes\*\shell\EasydictOCR` and `Software\Classes\Directory\Background\shell\EasydictOCR` command entries through the Rust helper, including the `Icon` named value, and unregister now deletes the verb tree.
- Kept protocol registration helpers in the same facade for the next coordinated desktop-integration slice, but did not change startup auto-registration behavior in this pass.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-registry/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app desktop_integration --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior shell_context_menu -- --nocapture`

- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers`

## 2026-06-13: Moved OCR capture task surface to a lib-owned Windows helper

- Continued the core OCR migration by moving the selected-region capture and window-snapshot task surface out of WinFluent platform tasks.
- Rechecked reusable Rust screen-capture crates before changing code. `windows-capture` is active and Windows Graphics Capture/DXGI oriented, while `xcap` and `screenshots` are cross-platform screenshot abstractions. Easydict's current OCR overlay needs a narrow synchronous BGRA BitBlt capture plus Win32 window hierarchy enumeration, so this slice uses Microsoft's official `windows` crate directly in `lib/easydict-windows-screen-capture` instead of adding a broader recording/cross-platform dependency.
- Added `easydict_app::screen_capture_native` as an unsafe-free app facade that converts between the helper DTOs and the existing app/WinFluent DTOs. OCR confirm/copy capture and capture-overlay window snapshots now emit Rust-owned `Task::perform(...)` futures rather than WinFluent `Task::CaptureScreenRegion` / `Task::CaptureScreenWindows`.
- Left UI-owned files alone. The overlay background-freeze helper in `state.rs` still calls the existing WinFluent platform capture path and remains a coordinated follow-up with the UI workstream.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-screen-capture/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app screen_capture_native --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test ocr_behavior capture -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test default_api_boundary_behavior default_process_spawn_surface_has_no_retained_dotnet_runtime_entries -- --nocapture`

- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers`

## 2026-06-13: Wired clipboard monitoring through an app-owned stream

- Continued the core clipboard migration by making saved `ClipboardMonitoring` settings start a Rust-owned monitor stream instead of relying on WinFluent `SubscriptionKind::Clipboard`, whose iced backend is still a no-op.
- No new clipboard crate was added. `clipboard-win` remains a viable crate for a future event-listener backend, but this slice can reuse the existing `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` Win32 clipboard sequence/snapshot helper and stay aligned with TextSelection/TextInsertion semantics.
- `easydict_app::clipboard` now owns a small polling monitor state machine: it seeds from the current clipboard, watches sequence changes, trims/ignores empty text, suppresses duplicate text, and ignores the app's own just-written clipboard text once.
- App startup starts a lazy `Task::stream(...)` only when the saved setting is enabled. Settings edits continue to respect the existing unsaved-changes model: toggling alone does not start/stop monitoring, while saving or discarding settings resynchronizes the monitor with the persisted settings state.
- Added source and behavior coverage proving the app uses `clipboard::clipboard_monitor_stream(...)` rather than WinFluent `Subscription::clipboard(...)`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app clipboard --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior monitor_clipboard -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior clipboard_monitor -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior settings_changes_prompt_on_back_and_save_discard_cancel_are_stateful -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`

## 2026-06-13: Centralized rs portable retained byte marker scanning

- Continued the first rs portable no-runtime hardening by moving the allowlisted-executable byte marker scanner out of `easydict_packager` and into `lib/easydict-runtime-guards`.
- No third-party crate is appropriate for this slice: the scanner is an Easydict-specific denylist for stale `.NET` host/runtime, retained worker, PowerShell, and old TTS script markers. Keeping it in `lib/` lets browser/runtime/package boundaries reuse one policy instead of re-copying byte tables.
- `validate-rs-portable` still rejects first-release allowlisted executables such as `Easydict.Rust.exe` and Rust helper `.exe` files if their bytes contain ASCII or UTF-16LE retained runtime markers, but the actual marker matching now calls `easydict_runtime_guards::bytes_contain_retained_runtime_marker(...)`.
- Added a packager source-boundary regression so the byte marker table and ASCII/UTF-16LE scanner are not re-inlined into `easydict_packager`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-runtime-guards/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-runtime-guards/Cargo.toml --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager marker -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_packager --all-targets`

## 2026-06-13: Moved runtime route policy into lib-owned guards

- Continued the default rs runtime cleanup by moving retained-worker runtime-profile parsing and disable-message policy out of `easydict_app::runtime_policy` and into `lib/easydict-runtime-guards`.
- No third-party crate is appropriate for this slice: the behavior is Easydict-specific runtime-profile policy, not a reusable parser concern. The existing lib boundary now owns both stale retained payload path classification and the runtime route policy gate.
- `easydict_app::runtime_policy` is now a thin re-export shim, preserving current Quick Translate, LongDoc, and retained-feature compatibility call sites while moving the implementation and feature-on policy matrix into `lib/`.
- Propagated the app `retained-dotnet-workers` feature to `easydict_runtime_guards/retained-dotnet-workers`, so compiling the retained bridge still requires the same explicit `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` opt-in before workers can be enabled.
- Added a default API boundary regression proving the app shim does not re-inline environment parsing or runtime policy implementation.

## 2026-06-13: Centralized retained payload path guards outside app code

- Continued shrinking default app production-source exposure to retained runtime markers without changing browser registration behavior.
- No third-party crate is appropriate for this slice: the logic is Easydict-specific policy over stale retained payload roots rather than generic path normalization. Added `lib/easydict-runtime-guards` as a small safe Rust library that classifies retained runtime/path components such as worker roots, legacy bridge names, and `.NET` payload roots.
- Updated browser native-messaging registrar source validation to call `easydict_runtime_guards::path_has_retained_runtime_component(...)` instead of inlining the marker list in `easydict_app::browser_registrar`. Existing protections still reject renamed `easydict-native-bridge.exe` files under stale retained payload directories.
- Added a browser registrar source-boundary guard so retained payload marker policy stays lib-owned.

## 2026-06-13: Moved app file/folder dialogs off PowerShell System.Windows.Forms

- Continued the default rs no-runtime cleanup on a core UI workflow boundary: LongDoc input selection, LongDoc output-folder selection, and MDX dictionary import no longer emit WinFluent `OpenFileDialog` / `OpenFolderDialog` tasks, whose iced backend still uses PowerShell plus `System.Windows.Forms`.
- Rechecked reusable dialog crates before changing code. `rfd` and `native-dialog` are mature cross-platform file-dialog crates, but this app only needs a narrow Windows portable boundary and must avoid script/runtime helpers. Added `lib/easydict-windows-dialogs` around Microsoft's official `windows` crate and the native COM `IFileOpenDialog` API instead.
- Added `easydict_app::file_dialog` as the app-owned facade. Dialog work runs on a dedicated STA thread, maps user cancel to `None`, supports file filters and initial folders, and keeps app crate unsafe-free.
- Added source/behavior coverage proving app update routes these user-driven file selections through Rust-owned futures instead of WinFluent dialog tasks.

## 2026-06-13: Moved explicit clipboard read/write off WinFluent tasks

- Continued the core input/clipboard migration without touching `state.rs`, `ui.rs`, or `lib/winfluent-rs`.
- Rechecked reusable clipboard crates before changing code. `clipboard-win` is the closest Windows-specific crate and includes optional monitoring support, while `arboard` is a mature cross-platform text/image clipboard abstraction. This slice needs exact reuse of the existing Windows clipboard semantics already implemented for TextSelection/TextInsertion, so it reuses `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` instead of adding a second clipboard dependency.
- Added `easydict_app::clipboard`, a small app-owned facade over the Rust Windows helper. Explicit tray `Translate Clipboard` reads now use a `Task::perform(...)` future that maps clipboard errors to `None` like the previous non-fatal UI behavior; result Copy and Silent OCR copy now write through the same Rust-owned helper and resolve to `Message::Noop` on completion.
- Added a source-boundary regression so the app core no longer emits WinFluent `Task::read_clipboard_text` or `Task::clipboard_text` for explicit clipboard actions. The later clipboard-monitoring slice wires the sustained monitor through an app-owned stream instead of WinFluent's no-op clipboard subscription.

## 2026-06-13: Reconfirmed Rust MDD support for the core MDX route

- Rechecked the user-requested MDD follow-up on the current dirty tree. The core Rust path is already implemented through the existing in-tree `lib/rs-mdict` fork and app-side `mdx_native` glue; no new parser dependency is needed.
- Confirmed support for MDD raw/base64 resource lookup, compressed and non-ASCII resources, cross-record-block reads, credential-encrypted companion MDD via the MDX key-header transform, same-stem `.mdd` / numbered `.N.mdd` discovery, multi-MDD first-hit lookup, failed-MDD skipping, WebView-ready `data:<mime>;base64,...` HTML inlining, and Quick Translate `rawHtml` retention only when MDD resources were actually inlined.
- The remaining MDD-related work is evidence/UX polish rather than core runtime migration: real large-dictionary/manual WebView screenshot review is still useful, but the Rust parser and Quick Translate behavior are covered by focused automated tests.

## 2026-06-13: Moved browser registrar registry access off WinFluent

- Continued shrinking default app dependencies on WinFluent platform internals by moving the Browser Native Messaging registrar registry adapter into `lib/easydict-windows-registry`.
- Rechecked the reusable option first. Microsoft `windows-registry` is a high-quality safe crate, but direct use would broaden deletion semantics to recursive tree removal and does not map as directly onto unnamed/default value handling. This helper therefore uses Microsoft's official `windows` crate directly to preserve the previous exact Win32 behavior: HKCU default `REG_SZ` read/write, non-recursive `RegDeleteKeyW`, missing key/value as a neutral success/miss.
- Updated `easydict_browser_registrar` so `SystemBrowserRegistry` calls the Rust-owned helper rather than `win_fluent_platform_win::WindowsPlatformAdapter`. Added a browser registrar source-boundary test to prevent the binary from reintroducing WinFluent registry access.
- Left UI, state, and WinFluent files untouched. The remaining non-UI direct WinFluent platform dependency in default app code is the screen-capture bridge in `state.rs`, which is owned by the parallel UI work and was not changed in this slice.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-registry/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test browser_registrar_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Moved native bridge named-event signaling off WinFluent

- Continued shrinking default app dependencies on WinFluent platform internals by moving the browser native-messaging OCR signal path into `lib/easydict-windows-ipc`.
- Rechecked reusable options first. Named events are a very narrow Win32 synchronization-object boundary, and the available higher-level Rust crates do not add value over the official `windows` crate for Easydict's exact `OpenEventW` / `SetEvent` semantics. The helper therefore exposes a small safe `signal_named_event(...) -> Result<bool, WindowsIpcError>` API, with a missing event preserving the existing `Ok(false)` behavior.
- Updated `easydict-native-bridge` to signal `Local\EasydictRs-OcrTranslate` through the Rust-owned helper instead of `win_fluent_platform_win::WindowsPlatformAdapter`. The browser stdio protocol, unknown-action no-signal behavior, and Rust helper naming remain unchanged.
- Left UI, state, and WinFluent files untouched. The remaining non-UI direct WinFluent platform dependency in default app code is the browser registrar registry adapter.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-ipc/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
