## 2026-06-13: Moved credential DPAPI boundary off WinFluent

- Continued removing default app dependencies on WinFluent platform internals by moving credential DPAPI and HKLM `MachineGuid` registry access into `lib/easydict-windows-credentials`.
- Rechecked reusable DPAPI crates first. `windows-dpapi` now exists as a small safe wrapper, but this path needs exact compatibility with Easydict's existing `edcred1` optional entropy, current-user/local-machine scope wire format, and machine-id fallback behavior, so the implementation uses Microsoft's official `windows` crate directly in a narrow in-tree helper.
- Updated `easydict_app::credential_protection` to call the new helper instead of `win_fluent_platform_win::WindowsPlatformAdapter`. The public credential format remains `edcred1:user|machine:<base64>`, legacy `edloc1:` AES-GCM migration remains unchanged, and app-side unsafe code stays out of `easydict_app`.
- Left UI, state, and WinFluent files untouched; remaining direct WinFluent platform uses outside UI/state are browser registrar registry helpers and native-bridge named-event signaling, which are good follow-up slices.

## 2026-06-13: Hardened Rust-only package scans for script/TTS runtime markers

- Followed up the Rust-owned TTS backend by hardening the release gates that prove rs portable/MSIX payloads do not carry the old script-based speech route. Rechecked PE parser options first: `goblin`, `object`, and `pelite` all support PE/COFF inspection, but this slice only needs fixed ASCII/UTF-16LE marker detection in package payload bytes, so the existing byte scanner was extended instead of adding a parser dependency.
- Extended `easydict_packager` rs portable validation markers beyond retained .NET host/runtime strings to include explicit script/TTS backend markers: `powershell.exe`, `pwsh.exe`, `System.Speech`, `System.Management.Automation`, and the old `WIN_FLUENT_TTS_TEXT` environment marker. Directory and ZIP portable validation both reject allowlisted helper executables containing those markers.
- Extended `easydict_msix_validate` rust-only payload scanning with the same markers. This covers direct MSIX validation, `prepare-package-inputs` preflight before manifest writing, and rust-only nested appx/msix validation inside MSIX bundles.

## 2026-06-13: Moved Quick Translate TTS off PowerShell/System.Speech

- Continued the core `.NET runtime` removal work on the TTS path. Rechecked reusable Rust TTS options first: `tts` provides a broad cross-platform abstraction but brings extra backend/event surface that Easydict does not need for first rs portable; `sapi-lite` is a small SAPI wrapper but has limited maintenance/coverage. The chosen route is a small in-tree wrapper under `lib/` using Microsoft's official `windows` crate and SAPI directly.
- Added `lib/easydict-windows-tts`, which initializes COM inside the helper, creates `ISpVoice`, optionally chooses an installed SAPI voice by BCP-47/LCID language match, and speaks plain text through the system default audio output without launching PowerShell or loading `System.Speech`.
- Added `easydict_app::tts`, a fakeable app-owned TTS boundary. Result Speak actions and `AutoPlayTranslation` now emit Rust `Task::perform(...)` futures instead of WinFluent `PlatformCommand::SpeakText`, so the app-side Quick Translate route no longer depends on the WinFluent PowerShell backend.
- Kept error handling fire-and-forget for parity with the previous platform side effect: empty text is skipped before backend calls, and native TTS errors resolve to `Message::Noop` so translation state is not disrupted. Real Notepad/app audio smoke remains pending for a later Windows ignored/manual pass.

## 2026-06-13: Moved TextInsertion replace backend into Rust-owned helper boundary

- Continued the core interaction migration without touching `ui.rs`, `state.rs`, `ui_contract.rs`, or WinFluent. Rechecked reuse options first: `clipboard-win` only covers clipboard operations, while `enigo`/`rdev` abstract keyboard simulation but do not preserve Easydict's HWND capture, foreground-window validation, thread-input attach, clipboard write, and `dwExtraInfo` requirements. The implementation therefore stays on the existing Microsoft `windows` crate helper in `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection`.
- Added Rust-native text insertion primitives next to TextSelection: foreground insertion-target capture, target validation, `Ctrl+V` SendInput, and `insert_text_into_target(...)` built from clipboard write + foreground restoration. Non-Windows remains an explicit unsupported boundary.
- Added `easydict_app::text_insertion`, a fakeable app-owned backend/cache for the last captured source window. `TranslateSelection`, PopButton click, Mini show hotkey, tray Translate Clipboard, and result Replace now use Rust `Task::perform(...)` helpers instead of WinFluent `PlatformCommand::CaptureTextInsertionTarget` / `InsertText`.
- Kept user-visible error handling conservative for this slice: insertion failures complete as `Message::Noop`, matching the previous fire-and-forget platform side effect shape. A later UI slice can surface failures without changing the helper boundary.

## 2026-06-13: Hardened Rust-only MSIX UTF-16LE .NET marker scan

- Reviewed PE inspection crate options before changing the release gate. `object`, `goblin`, and `pelite` all expose PE/COFF parsing, but this slice only needs known retained-runtime string detection across MSIX and prepared publish inputs, so the existing byte scanner was extended without adding a new dependency.
- `easydict_msix_validate` now scans Rust-only payload content markers in both ASCII and UTF-16LE encodings. This aligns MSIX/prepare-package-inputs with the existing `validate-rs-portable` protection and closes the renamed .NET apphost/resource-string variant before manifest writing or package validation succeeds.
- Added regressions for a helper executable and a `prepare-package-inputs` publish-dir file containing UTF-16LE `hostfxr.dll` / CoreLib markers.

Validation:

## 2026-06-13: Wired Rust Quick Translate auto-play TTS

- Continued with a small core UX closure that avoids the UI/state files currently being edited in parallel. The reusable-library check does not call for a new dependency here: Rust already has the platform-neutral `Task::speak_text` side-effect contract, and the backend maps it to the current Windows speech path.
- `EasydictApp::update(...)` now turns a successful `QuickTranslateServiceFinished` into a TTS platform task when `AutoPlayTranslation` is enabled. It matches the .NET behavior of playing only the first successful result for an active query, skips disabled/empty/error/stale updates, and uses the active surface target language for Main/Mini/Fixed windows.
- Streaming remains safe because only the final service-finished update can trigger auto-play; intermediate `QuickTranslateStreamChunk` messages do not speak.
- While running the full quick-translate suite, restored the encrypted MDX route predicate: `Encrypted=1` dictionaries with no credentials are still Rust-native candidates, so lookup fails locally with credentials-required instead of falling out of the native route. Invalid supplied credentials remain a local input error.
- Added the next MouseSelection app-boundary adapter without touching UI/state/WinFluent: producer `DismissPopButton` actions map to an app message task, `CaptureSelectionText` actions reuse the native selected-text capture task, and pending multi-click timer metadata was exposed at this step before the default runtime hook/timer wiring was completed later on 2026-06-13.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior auto_play_translation -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior encrypted_mdx -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior mouse_selection_producer -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Aligned unknown-process TextSelection fallback with .NET

- Closed a small but user-visible TextSelection parity gap found while reviewing the .NET strategy. When the foreground process name cannot be resolved, `.NET TextSelectionService` still classifies it as neither Electron nor terminal and therefore tries UIA first, then clipboard fallback with the standard timeout.
- Removed the Rust-only conservative UIA-only branch for unknown process names. Rust now treats that case like a regular desktop app: UIA first, then clipboard fallback; empty normalized process names still do not enter adaptive non-text suppression.
- Kept the change inside the existing Rust TextSelection strategy layer. No new dependency was needed; the native primitives remain in `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test text_selection_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Added Rust TextSelection UIA timeout guard

- Continued the core selected-text migration without touching UI/WinFluent. The reusable-library decision remains the same as the TextSelection backend foundation: `get-selected-text` is too narrow/clipboard-oriented for Easydict's terminal-safe flow, and `uiautomation` only covers UIA while the app also needs Win32 foreground, clipboard sequence, SendInput, and low-level hook primitives. The safe wrapper stays in `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` on Microsoft's `windows` crate.
- Added a Rust UIA execution timeout matching the .NET guard shape: one UIA capture at a time, a 200ms "busy" wait before skipping, and an 800ms execution timeout before returning a local `WindowsTextSelectionError` instead of blocking the hotkey/PopButton capture path indefinitely.
- Aligned the UIA pattern fallback with the .NET path: try `TextPattern` before `TextPattern2`, and treat an individual pattern failure as a miss so the next pattern or clipboard fallback can still run.
- Switched `NativeTextSelectionBackend` to the timeout-aware helper. Existing app strategy stays unchanged: regular desktop apps fall back to clipboard after UIA timeout/failure, Electron still tries clipboard first and avoids double Ctrl+C, and terminal apps remain UIA-only so a timeout never sends SIGINT.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-text-selection/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test text_selection_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Added WindowsNative OCR RecognizeAsync timeout/cancel guard

- Continued the Rust-native OCR hardening after the image-dimension guard. The reusable-library check remains unchanged: Windows Native OCR is already best served by Microsoft `windows` / `windows-future`; third-party OCR engines such as Tesseract/leptess would replace the engine and add native packaging, while generic wrappers would not improve Easydict's DTO/language/fallback contract.
- `recognize_bgra_file(...)` no longer blocks indefinitely on `RecognizeAsync(...).join()`. It registers a `windows-future` completion callback with `when(...)`, waits on a standard channel with a 30s default timeout, and calls `Cancel()` on timeout before returning a local Rust `WindowsOcrError`.
- Kept the public API stable and did not introduce an async runtime or new dependency. The timeout is intentionally generous for screenshot OCR and leaves normal completion behavior unchanged.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_windows_ocr -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test ocr_behavior -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_windows_ocr --all-targets`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers`

## 2026-06-13: Added Rust low-level mouse selection hook foundation

- Continued the PopButton/mouse-selection migration after TextSelection capture was wired. Two read-only subagents confirmed the remaining gap is the real `WH_MOUSE_LL` / `WH_KEYBOARD_LL` producer and its app message adapter; the PopButton consumer path (`SelectionTextReady`, auto-dismiss, click-to-Mini translation) already exists.
- Rechecked reusable hook crates before changing code. `willhook` wraps Windows low-level hooks and `rdev` exposes cross-platform global input events, but both abstract away details Easydict needs to preserve for parity: raw Win32 mouse/keyboard message ids, `dwExtraInfo` for `EASYDICT_SYNTHETIC_KEY`, exact event timestamps, and point-to-PopButton-root hit-testing. The implementation therefore stays in `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` on the official Microsoft `windows` crate instead of adding a third-party hook dependency.
- Added `LowLevelInputHookEvent` plus a Windows `start_low_level_input_hook()` helper that installs `WH_MOUSE_LL` and `WH_KEYBOARD_LL` on a background message-loop thread, forwards raw hook events through a channel, and unhooks/joins on drop. Non-Windows remains an explicit unsupported boundary.
- Added small platform helpers for `GetDoubleClickTime`, `GetTickCount64`, and `WindowFromPoint`/`GetAncestor(GA_ROOT)` root hit-testing so the later PopButton producer can keep the same double-click timing and "clicking the PopButton itself does not dismiss" behavior as the .NET service.
- Added an app-side raw-event reducer in `mouse_selection.rs` that feeds the new helper events into the existing pure `MouseSelectionHookState` without touching UI/state files. Mouse events now reuse drag/multi-click planning, while keyboard events reuse the synthetic Ctrl+C dismiss guard.
- Added a pure `MouseSelectionProducer` action layer on top of the reducer. It owns monotonic PopButton generations, converts drag selections into `CaptureSelectionText` requests, converts double/triple-click detections into cancellable pending timer actions, ignores stale pending generations, and exposes a `MouseSelectionTextReady` shape matching the existing `SelectionTextReady` app message fields. This lets the later runtime subscription map actions to `DismissPopButton`, timer tasks, selection capture, and `SelectionTextReady` without adding unsafe code or UI dependencies to `easydict_app`.
- Added `mouse_selection_capture_result_message(...)` and `mouse_selection_capture_task(...)` at the app boundary. A producer `CaptureSelectionText` request can now reuse the existing native selected-text capture future and maps nonblank output directly into the existing `SelectionTextReady` PopButton consumer; blank/failed capture maps to `Noop`.
- Remaining work: wire this helper into an actual app subscription/background producer, schedule pending multi-click timers in the runtime task layer, and dispatch producer actions to `DismissPopButton`, `mouse_selection_capture_task(...)`, and timer completion. Real Notepad/VS Code/Windows Terminal smoke coverage is still pending.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-text-selection/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test mouse_selection_behavior -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior mouse_selection_capture -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers`

## 2026-06-13: Added WindowsNative OCR MaxImageDimension guard

- Continued the Rust-native OCR hardening slice after the availability/status boundary. A read-only subagent checked the `windows 0.62.2` bindings and the .NET reference path; Microsoft exposes `OcrEngine::MaxImageDimension()` as the documented per-side image limit for `Windows.Media.Ocr`, while `RecognizeAsync` cancellation/timeout parity should be a later API-shape decision rather than part of this small guard.
- Kept the implementation inside the existing `easydict_windows_ocr` helper crate. No new OCR dependency was added: Tesseract/leptess remain heavier engine replacements, while the current Rust wrapper already targets Easydict's Windows Native OCR contract through Microsoft's `windows` WinRT bindings.
- `recognize_bgra_file(...)` now creates the OCR engine first, preserving the existing "no engine available -> empty result" behavior, then checks `OcrEngine::MaxImageDimension()` before allocating `SoftwareBitmap` or calling `RecognizeAsync`. Images whose width or height exceeds the engine limit now fail locally with a clear Rust-native OCR error instead of depending on WinRT allocation/recognition failure.
- Added pure crate tests for `width == max` / `height == max` acceptance, width/height over-limit rejection, and invalid zero engine-limit handling. The broader app OCR behavior tests still prove WindowsNative/Ollama/Custom API routes do not probe legacy OCR workers or CompatHost.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_windows_ocr -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test ocr_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Hardened WindowsNative OCR availability for portable rs

- Rechecked OCR replacement options before changing code. `leptess`/Tesseract are mature general OCR bindings but bring Tesseract/Leptonica/native language-data packaging; `oneocr-rs` targets the Windows 11 Snipping Tool engine rather than the documented Easydict behavior; thin Windows OCR wrappers such as `win_ocr` do not cover Easydict's DTO/language/fallback contracts better than the current in-tree wrapper. The existing Rust-owned `easydict_windows_ocr` crate on Microsoft `windows` WinRT bindings remains the best fit for Windows Native OCR parity.
- Microsoft documents `Windows.Media.Ocr` as a package-identity API for desktop apps, which matters for the first rs portable build. The Rust app already runs WindowsNative OCR locally and does not probe legacy `workers/ocr`/CompatHost; this slice tightens the availability/status path so portable/unavailable environments report `isAvailable=false` with an empty language list instead of attempting `AvailableRecognizerLanguages()` after the engine is unavailable.
- Added `windows_native_ocr_availability_skips_language_query_when_unavailable` to lock that behavior through the fake recognizer. This keeps settings/diagnostics callers on a clear Rust-native unavailable boundary and avoids resurrecting any `.NET` OCR worker fallback.

## 2026-06-13: Wired Rust TextSelection native backend into Translate Selection

- Continued the core input/selection migration after the MDD route was verified. A read-only subagent audit confirmed the critical gap was not the planner but the missing app event wiring and foreground process-name context needed to avoid sending Ctrl+C to terminals.
- Kept the backend on the official Microsoft `windows` crate inside the new `lib/easydict-windows-text-selection` helper crate. `uiautomation` remains useful for UIA-only experiments, but this slice also needs foreground HWND/PID, process-image lookup, clipboard sequence polling, clipboard restore, thread-input focus, and `SendInput`; generic `get-selected-text` crates do not cover Easydict's Electron/terminal/suppression contract.
- Added `process_name_for_id(...)` to the helper crate so Rust can classify the foreground target using `.NET Process.ProcessName`-style stems. If process-name resolution fails, the Rust planner now takes a conservative UIA-only route rather than risking a clipboard fallback into a terminal.
- Added a native capture wrapper with a shared suppression tracker in `text_selection.rs`: foreground target discovery, process classification, UIA attempt, clipboard fallback with ClipWait/restore, and success/non-text suppression recording now run behind the same fakeable planner tested earlier.
- Wired `Message::TranslateSelection` and the settings "Translate Selection" hotkey to capture the insertion target and then run the Rust-native selected-text future. Non-empty captured text reuses the existing quick-translate entry point; the tray's explicit "Translate Clipboard" command remains on the old read-clipboard task.
- Remaining TextSelection work: real mouse-hook producer to feed `SelectionTextReady` for PopButton, Windows ignored/E2E coverage against Notepad/terminal apps, and a timeout guard around synchronous UIA calls to match the .NET 200ms/800ms safeguards.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-windows-text-selection/Cargo.toml --all-targets`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test text_selection_behavior -- --nocapture`

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior tray_commands_route_to_existing_desktop_actions -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --all-targets`

## 2026-06-13: Hardened raw retained WorkerCommand startup guard

- Continued the first rs portable/runtime boundary hardening. No new library was needed; this is an internal retained-worker feature guard rather than a replacement of parser/OCR/Win32 functionality.
- `compat_client::WorkerCommand::spawn(...)` now classifies retained worker/runtime marker paths even when callers construct the command directly with `WorkerCommand::new(...)` instead of the packaged helper. Paths under `workers/<kind>`, `Easydict.Workers.*` executables, `dotnet.exe`, hostfxr/hostpolicy/CoreCLR DLLs, and `dotnet` path components all require the same explicit `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` opt-in before any executable probing or deferred `.NET` runtime env injection.
- Added feature-gated `compat_client` regressions proving raw LongDoc, LocalAI, unknown worker, and dotnet runtime commands fail locally with Rust-native-route-required when the profile is unset, while explicit `hybrid` still reaches the normal missing-executable I/O boundary for coexistence tests.

- `cargo test --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_app --features retained-dotnet-workers --test compat_client raw_worker_command -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-13: Added Rust TextSelection pure strategy core

- Rechecked reusable Windows selection/clipboard crates before changing code. `uiautomation` is a useful Windows UI Automation wrapper, but this slice only migrates Easydict's app-specific strategy planner; `get-selected-text` style crates are too generic for the terminal/Electron/suppression/clipboard-restore contract. No new dependency was added; the later Win32/UIA backend can stay on the official `windows` crate boundary already used elsewhere.
- Added Rust `text_selection` pure logic for process-name normalization, Electron and terminal classification, target selection planning, ClipWait payload classification, per-process non-text suppression, clipboard restore decisions, synthetic Ctrl+C key input planning, and a small attempt-result reducer.
- Added `text_selection_behavior`, mirroring the .NET `TextSelectionServiceTests` strategy boundaries: Electron clipboard-first route, terminal UIA-only route, ordinary UIA-then-clipboard route, own-process skip, two-tick non-text ClipWait, suppression expiry/recovery/exemptions, unknown-process non-suppression, modifier flushing before Ctrl+C, `EASYDICT_SYNTHETIC_KEY` marker propagation, and final outcome recording for success/failed clipboard attempts.

## 2026-06-12: Added Rust encrypted-MDD key-header transform support

- Split the user-requested MDD follow-up with read-only subagents: app-side companion discovery/resource inlining is already wired, while the library audit found one real core gap in `lib/rs-mdict`: MDD did not expose the same key-header transform entry point that MDX already had.
- Rechecked reusable parser options before changing code. `mdict-rs` advertises defensive MDX/MDD parsing but is AGPL-3.0-only; `readmdict` is MIT but direct-port style; `libmdx` advertises broader features but is AGPL-3.0. The existing MIT in-tree `lib/rs-mdict` remains the lowest-risk route.
- Added `Mdd::new_with_key_header_transform(...)`, reusing `MdictBase::new_with_key_header_transform(...)`, so encrypted companion MDD files can be opened by callers that already own the credential-derived decryptor. Without the transform, encrypted MDD still fails locally with `EncryptedFileRequiresPasscode` instead of being treated as a silent miss.
- Updated the app-side `RsMdictMddReaderFactory` to receive the MDX dictionary snapshot and reuse the existing MDX credential-derived key-header decryptor for companion MDD files. Plain MDDs still open through the unchanged `Mdd::new(...)` route.
- Added generated encrypted-MDD fixtures covering the public constructor, caller transform, encrypted record-block decrypt path, resolved resource key, raw bytes, MIME inference, and HTML data-URL inlining through the production app MDD factory.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml new_with_key_header_transform_reads_record_encrypted_mdd_resource -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test mdx_native_behavior native_mdd_resource_lookup_reads_record_encrypted_mdd_with_dictionary_credentials -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test mdx_native_behavior mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test settings_storage_behavior mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml --check`
- `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check rs/crates/easydict_app/src/mdx_native.rs rs/crates/easydict_app/tests/mdx_native_behavior.rs`

- Changed the workflow input to a blank string default and removed the `|| 'hybrid'` environment fallback. The existing validation step now fails omitted/blank values and only continues after the caller enters `hybrid`.

- `validate-rs-portable` now scans allowlisted executable bytes for both ASCII and UTF-16LE encodings of the retained .NET host/runtime markers, closing the rename-and-wide-string variant for apphost-style payloads inside `Easydict.Rust.exe` or Rust helper names.

## 2026-06-12: Locked Windows Native OCR language fallback order

- Rechecked OCR replacement options before changing code. No new dependency was added: Windows Native OCR stays on the official `windows` crate / WinRT `Windows.Media.Ocr` path, while Tesseract/leptess would add native engine, language-data, and packaging surface that does not fit the first rs portable target.
- `easydict_windows_ocr` now has a pure engine-attempt planner that trims a preferred language tag, ignores empty/`auto`, tries the preferred recognizer first, and always falls back to `TryCreateFromUserProfileLanguages()` afterward.

## 2026-06-12: Revalidated Rust MDD support and restored MDX credential preflight parity

- Rechecked available MDict/MDD libraries before changing code. `mdict-rs` is the only newer Rust library-first candidate that advertises MDX/MDD support, but switching parser crates would add license/API and behavior risk for no immediate core gap; `readmdict` is MIT but is a Python-origin route, and `mdict-parser` still does not implement MDD. The existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork remains the selected reusable library.
- Confirmed the current Rust route already covers the core MDD path: `Mdd` raw/base64 resource lookup, MIME inference, zlib/non-ASCII generated fixtures, cross-record-block reads, same-stem companion `.mdd` discovery, first-hit multi-MDD lookup, failed-MDD skipping, HTML data-URL inlining, and Quick Translate rich HTML separation.
- Fixed one adjacent MDX preflight regression found while validating the MDD suite: record-block encrypted dictionaries with present but invalid regcode/email now report the local Base64/key-length credential error before opening the native reader, while unsupported `Encrypted=3` / unknown modes still fail locally as unsupported before any CompatHost path.

## 2026-06-12: Added Rust-native mouse selection gesture core and tightened LongDoc helper resolution

- Rechecked reusable hook libraries before changing code. `willhook` and `rdev` can wrap global input hooks, but this slice only migrates the pure MouseHook state machine, so no new dependency was added; later Win32 hook integration can stay on the existing `windows` crate boundary.
- Added `mouse_selection` with Rust-owned drag detection, multi-click detection, hook-message outcome planning, PopButton self-click dismissal guard, excluded-app suppression, and the `EASYDICT_SYNTHETIC_KEY` keyboard marker used to ignore synthetic Ctrl+C.
- Added `mouse_selection_behavior`, mirroring the .NET `MouseHookServiceTests` thresholds: 10px drag distance, 4px multi-click distance, double/triple-click timing, drag cancellation of pending multi-click, scroll/right/key dismiss semantics, and synthetic-key suppression.
- Hardened `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1` helper resolution so every resolved helper path, including explicit `-RustHelperPath`, `-AppDir\easydict_long_doc.exe`, source target candidates, and PATH discovery, is rejected if it lives under retained `dotnet/`, `workers/`, `host/fxr/`, shared runtime, `Easydict.CompatHost*`, `Easydict.NativeBridge*`, or `Easydict.Workers.*` payload roots. A file renamed to `easydict_long_doc.exe` can no longer bypass the leaf-name guard.

## 2026-06-12: Reconfirmed MDD rs support and tightened LongDoc/browser no-runtime boundaries

- No new dependency was needed. MDD remains on the existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork, which is already the best-fit in-tree route for the required MDX/MDD semantics.
- Re-ran the focused MDD suites after the user-requested check. The current Rust path covers MDD parsing, raw/base64 resource lookup, cross-record-block reads, companion `.mdd` discovery, MIME inference, WebView-ready data-URL inlining, first-hit ordering across multiple MDD files, failed-MDD skipping, and Quick Translate rich HTML separation.
- Moved native LongDoc output/result JSON preflight ahead of PDF source reader/OCR fallback. If a PDF sidecar/output target is a directory, the run now fails locally before PDFium/source extraction, content-stream fallback, OCR, provider translation, or partial output writes.
- Hardened the browser registrar source guard so renamed `easydict-native-bridge.exe` candidates are rejected not only from `workers/` and `dotnet/`, but also from legacy `Easydict.CompatHost`, `Easydict.NativeBridge`, and `Easydict.Workers.*` payload directories, including canonical target checks.
- Tightened the browser bridge default wording: registrar usage/help now names `easydict_browser_registrar`, native bridge fatal stderr uses the `easydict-native-bridge` prefix, and the default boundary scan rejects legacy `BrowserHostRegistrar` / `Easydict NativeBridge` markers.
- Added LongDoc PowerShell shim evidence for the remaining native options: `-EnvFile`, `-Layout`, `-MaxConcurrency`, and `-Vision*` settings are forwarded to the Rust helper under a forced rust-only child environment without invoking cargo/dotnet tools.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test mdx_native_behavior mdd -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app native_pdf_result_json_path_prechecked_before_source_reader_or_ocr --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_behavior native_pdf_result_json_path_prechecked_before_provider_translation -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_behavior native_pdf_both_output_prechecks_bilingual_text_path_before_writing_pdf_file -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test browser_registrar_behavior install_rejects_renamed_bridge_sources_from_legacy_payload_roots -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test browser_registrar_behavior browser_registrar_usage_names_rust_binary_not_legacy_alias -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test native_bridge_behavior native_bridge_binary_signals_rs_ocr_event_not_legacy_dotnet_event -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test native_bridge_behavior native_bridge_binary_handles_unknown_action_without_dotnet_host_or_event_signal -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test default_api_boundary_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Tightened MDX/MDD credentials, discovery, and CLI no-runtime contracts

- No new dependency was needed. The MDX/MDD work stays on the existing local `lib/rs-mdict` fork and the app-owned credential/settings glue; the CLI guards reuse existing Rust-native route tests.
- Fixed record-block encrypted MDX routing so native lookup is allowed only when both credentials are present and the registration code passes local validation. Without credentials, the route now stays credential-gated instead of being treated as plain native lookup.
- Settings load now preserves saved MDD paths while appending newly discovered same-stem companion MDD files. This keeps user-added external MDDs and picks up later `Demo.1.mdd` / `Demo.4.mdd` resources without requiring manual settings edits.
- Added a CLI unsupported-language guard proving `easydict_cli translate --service google --to auto` fails during Rust language preflight before provider HTTP and without retained worker, CompatHost, or `.NET` wording.
- Strengthened LongDoc CLI contracts: `--list-services` includes canonical `windows-local-ai` without stale LocalAI aliases, and legacy `--app-dir` remains a no-op even when both runtime profile env vars are `hybrid`.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test mdx_native_behavior native_mdx_record_encrypted_dictionary_still_requires_credentials -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test settings_storage_behavior settings_storage_load_merges_discovered_companion_mdds_with_saved_paths -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test cli_translate_behavior native_google_cli_rejects_target_auto_before_provider_http_or_worker_lookup -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_cli_behavior list_services_succeeds_without_document_arguments -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_cli_behavior app_dir_ignores_stale_dotnet_payload_markers_and_does_not_enable_worker_lookup -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app --check`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_app --all-targets`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_app --all-targets --features retained-dotnet-workers`

## 2026-06-12: Made Linguee a default Rust-native Quick Translate service

- No new dependency was needed. The existing Rust `traditional_http` request planner, Reqwest executor, Linguee parser, and local endpoint override already cover the service.
- Moved Linguee out of the core `enable-linguee-service` gate: the default service catalog now registers `linguee`, and `traditional_http_config_for_service("linguee", ...)` returns the native keyless Linguee config in default builds.
- Converted the Linguee catalog/native-route/CLI contracts from feature-only to default-build coverage, proving `easydict_cli translate --service linguee --json` can execute against a local HTTP server and stays free of retained worker, CompatHost, or `.NET` wording.
- The old feature remains available for UI/compatibility slices; this change only moves the core catalog and provider route onto the default Rust path.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test translation_services_behavior service_catalog -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test traditional_http_behavior traditional_http_config_routes_linguee_by_default -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior default_linguee_catalog_entry_routes_to_native_traditional_http -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test cli_translate_behavior native_linguee_cli_succeeds_against_local_api_without_worker_or_compat_host_wording -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app --check`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_app --all-targets`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_app --all-targets --features enable-linguee-service`

## 2026-06-12: Added default DeepL Web CLI no-runtime contract

- No new dependency was needed. This reuses the existing DeepL Web JSON-RPC planner/parser and the debug-only traditional HTTP endpoint override pattern.
- Added `EASYDICT_TEST_TRADITIONAL_HTTP_ENDPOINT_DEEPL_WEB` for debug/test builds so the real CLI binary can exercise the default DeepL Web route against a local HTTP server without touching the live provider. Release builds keep the normal `https://www2.deepl.com/jsonrpc` endpoint.
- Added a CLI regression for `easydict_cli translate --service deepl --json` with an empty settings directory, proving the default mode is native DeepL Web JSON-RPC rather than the official API or any retained worker path.
- The test asserts the JSON-RPC method, text, source/target language fields, successful parsed output, and absence of retained worker, CompatHost, or `.NET` wording.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test cli_translate_behavior native_deepl_web_cli_uses_default_web_mode_without_worker_or_compat_host_wording -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Promoted no-runtime smoke coverage into CI and release gates

- No new dependency was needed. This reuses the existing Rust behavior tests and release-contract checks.
- The rs portable release gate now runs retained-feature Quick Translate and LongDoc behavior tests, proving that even when `retained-dotnet-workers` is compiled and a worker policy is injected, retained workers still require explicit `hybrid` runtime opt-in.
- The default CI `rust-only-boundary` job now runs a real fake-cargo rs portable ZIP payload validation plus CLI/LongDoc stale `--app-dir` payload smokes, so PRs catch regressions before tag release.
- Added a CLI black-box regression for `easydict_cli translate` without `--service`; the default Google route hits the Rust-native HTTP path and does not emit retained runtime, worker, CompatHost, dotnet, PowerShell, pwsh, or hostfxr wording.
- The legacy `.NET` publish script still supports `-CreateZip` for explicit hybrid coexistence, but now names that archive `Easydict-legacy-hybrid-win-...zip` so it cannot be confused with the first rs portable ZIP.

## 2026-06-12: Hardened Rust-only helper and extension package ingress points

- No new dependency was needed. This closes two read-only subagent/runtime-boundary findings with local denylist and filesystem-entry checks.
- `package-browser-extension` now rejects symlink or Windows reparse-point source entries for manifest/common allowlisted files before creating the ZIP/XPI archive, so a linked `background.js`, icon, or locale file cannot smuggle a stale `.NET` payload into the browser extension package.
- `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1 -RustHelperPath` now rejects explicit retained `.NET` runtime/worker/CompatHost command names (`dotnet.*`, `Easydict.Workers.*`, `Easydict.CompatHost*`) before spawn, while preserving fake/custom Rust helper names. `-UseCargo` remains cargo-first and ignores a retained `-RustHelperPath` value instead of resolving or spawning it.

## 2026-06-12: Provisioned WindowsAI WinMD metadata for hybrid helper builds

- No new dependency was needed. Hybrid release and ARM64 MSIX smoke now use the same official `Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.AI` NuGet metadata preparation step that the rs portable release already uses.
- `publish-msix` and `arm64-msix-smoke` export `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_APP_SDK_AI_METADATA_DIR` and `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_AI_REQUIRE_WINRT_BINDINGS=1` before `Build-RustHelpers.ps1`, so strict `lib/easydict-windows-ai` binding generation does not depend on runner cache state.

## 2026-06-12: Blocked Windows command shims in the Foundry Local CLI boundary

- No new dependency was needed. This tightens the existing `lib/easydict-foundry-local` denylist for the one default rs process boundary that may spawn an external native CLI.
- `EASYDICT_FOUNDRY_LOCAL_CLI` now rejects Windows shim forms such as `dotnet.cmd`, `dotnet.bat`, `powershell.cmd`, `powershell.bat`, `pwsh.cmd`, and `pwsh.bat`, alongside the already blocked `.exe` names and retained worker/runtime paths.

## 2026-06-12: Moved hidden .NET payload checks earlier in Rust-only packaging

- No new dependency was needed. The MSIX prepare path now reuses the existing ASCII-insensitive `.NET` host/runtime content marker scan that final MSIX and bundle validation already use.
- `prepare-package-inputs` now scans regular publish-dir files in Rust-only mode before writing the prepared manifest. A renamed apphost or support file with `hostfxr.dll`, CoreLib, runtimeconfig/deps, CompatHost, or worker markers is rejected even when its path does not look like `dotnet/` or `workers/`.
- `publish-rs-portable` now runs the existing CLI and LongDoc `--app-dir` stale-payload regressions before building the ZIP, and `release_contract_behavior` locks those workflow commands so the release gate cannot silently drop them.

## 2026-06-12: Added default CI and Cargo-bin guards for Rust-only entrypoints

- Two read-only subagent audits looked for the next non-UI runtime boundary gaps. No new dependency was needed; both fixes reuse existing Cargo manifest and release-contract checks.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` now has a `rust-only-boundary` job that forces `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, then runs the packager CI self-check, `easydict_app runtime_policy --lib`, and `default_api_boundary_behavior` without enabling retained worker features or setting up .NET.
- `easydict_app` now sets `autobins = false` and explicitly lists the app, CLI, LongDoc, NativeBridge, BrowserRegistrar, and LexIndex bins. The default API boundary test enumerates `src/bin/*.rs` so a future helper cannot become a default rs executable just by being added to the directory.

## 2026-06-12: Added gated real-provider smoke entries for native PDF layout

- No new dependency was needed. The smoke entries reuse the existing Rust `ort` DocLayout-YOLO/TATR wrappers and the current `ReqwestVisionLayoutHttpClient` Vision LLM executor.
- Added default-skipped, env-gated tests for the real provider boundaries: `EASYDICT_DOC_LAYOUT_YOLO_ONNX_SMOKE_RUNTIME_DIR` + `EASYDICT_DOC_LAYOUT_YOLO_ONNX_SMOKE_MODEL`, `EASYDICT_TATR_ONNX_SMOKE_RUNTIME_DIR` + `EASYDICT_TATR_ONNX_SMOKE_MODEL`, and `EASYDICT_VISION_LAYOUT_SMOKE_ENDPOINT` + `EASYDICT_VISION_LAYOUT_SMOKE_MODEL` + optional `EASYDICT_VISION_LAYOUT_SMOKE_API_KEY`.
- When the env vars are absent, the tests print a skip message and do not load models or call a network endpoint. When configured, they load/run the provider on synthetic BGRA document/table images and assert finite in-bounds detections or table structures.
- This closes the missing executable smoke entry for native PDF layout enrichment while leaving real-corpus parity gates and Settings UI button wiring as follow-up work.

Validation:

## 2026-06-12: Hardened explicit LongDoc PDF overlay font selection

- No new dependency was needed. The explicit `SettingsSnapshot.cjk_font_path` path now reuses the existing `ttf-parser` based managed CJK font validation from `font_download`.
- LongDoc PDF overlay still requires explicit CJK fonts to live under the configured/default Rust-managed `Fonts` cache and use a known NotoSans CJK asset filename, but now also rejects corrupt or non-CJK files even when the name and directory look correct.
- This aligns explicit font selection with automatic cache fallback, so a first rs portable run cannot be tricked into embedding an arbitrary or broken font file during native PDF overlay export.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app long_document::tests::native_pdf_overlay_font_rejects_invalid_explicit_managed_cjk_font_path --lib -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Moved default Foundry runtime construction behind the lib boundary

- Rechecked the reusable provider first. The official Microsoft `foundry-local-sdk` adapter already lives under `lib/easydict-foundry-local` as an opt-in feature, so this slice did not add another SDK wrapper or dependency.
- `lib/easydict-foundry-local` now exposes `DefaultFoundryLocalRuntimeController` plus `default_foundry_local_runtime_controller()`. The default no-SDK build still uses the existing CLI-backed controller, while SDK-enabled builds keep SDK selection inside the lib.
- Quick Translate, streaming Quick Translate, LongDoc native LocalAI probing, CLI native probing, and the app Foundry prepare task now construct the default Foundry controller through the lib-owned factory instead of directly naming the CLI resolver.
- The app crate gained explicit opt-in `foundry-sdk` / `foundry-sdk-winml` feature plumbing to the lib, while `default = []` remains unchanged for the first rs portable package.

## 2026-06-12: Provisioned official WindowsAI WinMD metadata for rs portable releases

- Rechecked the source before changing CI. The official `Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.AI` NuGet package is the right upstream for Windows App SDK AI WinMD metadata; no Rust dependency was added.
- `publish-rs-portable` now downloads the latest stable package from NuGet flat container, extracts only the package contents on the runner, verifies `Microsoft.Windows.AI.winmd`, `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Foundation.winmd`, and `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text.winmd`, then exports `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_APP_SDK_AI_METADATA_DIR` and `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_AI_REQUIRE_WINRT_BINDINGS=1` through `GITHUB_ENV`.
- The metadata step runs before release contract tests and before `Package-Portable.ps1`, so strict WindowsAI bindings fail in CI instead of silently shipping the unsupported fallback.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior rs_portable_release_provisions_windows_ai_winmd_metadata_before_cargo -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Hardened Rust-only archives against hidden .NET runtime payloads

- No new dependency was needed. This reuses `zip` crate archive metadata, existing ASCII-insensitive runtime marker scans, and the current packager/MSIX validators.
- Rust-only MSIX validation now scans every non-directory payload file for .NET host/runtime markers, not just `.exe` entries, and rejects archive symlink entries before payload allowlist checks. The same policy is applied to nested packages inside MSIX bundles.
- `prepare-package-inputs` now fails before writing the prepared manifest when a Rust-only publish directory contains any symlink or Windows reparse-point payload entry, including safe-looking `Assets/...` names.
- `validate-rs-portable` now rejects symlink entries inside the portable ZIP before required/allowed payload checks, so the first rs portable archive cannot hide retained `.NET` runtime files through links.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_packager --all-targets`

## 2026-06-12: Expanded Rust-only MSIX apphost marker scanning to all payload executables

- No new dependency was needed. Reused the existing marker-based renamed-apphost scan and made it ASCII case-insensitive while indexing package payloads.
- Rust-only MSIX and nested bundle validation now scan every payload `.exe`, not only required Rust helper names, so an extra `tools/*.exe` or `assets/*.exe` cannot smuggle a renamed .NET apphost into the first rs package path.
- Added ordinary MSIX and bundle regressions for non-helper payload executables with mixed-case .NET host/runtime markers.

## 2026-06-12: Added native LongDoc PDF backfill metrics to quality reports

- No new dependency was needed. The native PDF exporter already returns content-stream replacement and overlay/font-embedding summaries; the LongDoc result builder now maps those into the retained-worker-compatible `BackfillQualityMetrics` field names.
- PDF content-stream replacement now reports `candidateBlocks`, `renderedBlocks`, and `objectReplaceBlocks` in `qualityReport.backfillMetrics`; overlay/font embedding and content-stream no-match overlay fallback report `overlayModeBlocks`.
- The same `qualityReport` JSON string is returned in `TranslateDocumentResult` and persisted in the Rust-native result sidecar.

## 2026-06-12: Added native LongDoc quality reports to result JSON sidecars

- No new dependency was needed. This reuses the existing Rust-native LongDoc checkpoint/source-chunk metadata and serializes the same high-level `LongDocumentQualityReport` shape used by retained worker results.
- Native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF runs now populate `TranslateDocumentResult.qualityReport` with a JSON string containing `stageTimingsMs`, `backfillMetrics`, `totalBlocks`, `translatedBlocks`, `skippedBlocks`, and `failedBlocks`.
- Retry Failed now rebuilds the report from the updated checkpoint before rewriting the sidecar, so a completed retry clears the failed-block diagnostics instead of preserving the initial partial report.

## 2026-06-12: Kept encrypted MDX Quick Translate on the Rust-native route

- Rechecked reusable-library options for the MDD/MDX slice before changing code. Existing `lib/rs-mdict` remains the best fit for this repo because it already owns the migrated MDD resource reader, MDX lookup, encryption primitives, and Quick Translate integration; no new dependency was added.
- `native_mdx_dictionary_can_route_natively` now treats `Encrypted=1` record-block dictionaries without credentials as Rust-native candidates when the header mode itself is supported. The actual lookup still returns a local credentials-required error before opening the reader.
- Invalid regcodes, missing encrypted files, and unsupported combined encryption modes still fail locally with the existing native input errors. The route split prevents default rs Quick Translate from falling back to CompatHost/.NET for the supported encrypted MDX mode while preserving the stricter bad-input matrix.

## 2026-06-12: Enriched Quick Translate cache hits with native Youdao phonetics

- No new dependency was needed; this reuses the existing Rust-native Youdao phonetic enrichment path and in-memory phonetic cache.
- Quick Translate cache hits that translate into English and are missing target phonetics now route through a phonetic-only task before emitting `QuickTranslateServiceFinished`. The task does not start the translation provider, retained worker, or stream path.
- Non-phonetic cache hits still return a direct message, preserving the fast cache-hit behavior for normal repeated queries.
- Added `quick_translate_cache_hit_enriches_missing_english_phonetics_without_provider_task`, which seeds the native phonetic cache, hits the translation cache, executes the returned future, and verifies phonetics are merged while no provider cache miss is registered.

- Rechecked replacement options before touching the path. The local `lib/rs-mdict` fork is already the Rust implementation under migration, and previous scouting found no better licensed drop-in replacement for the current MDX/MDD behavior; no new dependency was added.
- Added `native_quick_translate_reads_real_mdx_and_inlines_real_mdd_resources`, which creates real minimal MDX and MDD fixtures, runs the default Quick Translate native route, and proves `translatedText` stays plain text while `rawHtml` carries an MDD `data:image/png;base64,...` URL without CompatHost/.NET wording.
- Added `native_mdd_html_inline_decodes_utf8_percent_encoded_resource_paths`, covering UTF-8 percent-encoded CJK MDD paths such as `%E6%A0%87%E5%BF%97.png`.

## 2026-06-12: Covered unknown legacy packaging runtime profiles before tool invocation

- No new dependency was needed; this extends the existing fake-`PATH` release contract around legacy PowerShell packaging scripts.
- `legacy_packaging_scripts_reject_non_hybrid_profiles_before_invoking_external_tools` now executes `dotnet/scripts/publish.ps1`, `package-and-install.ps1`, `Package-Msix.ps1`, `Build-Installer.ps1`, and `Extract-DotnetRuntime.ps1` with omitted, `rust-only`, old `dotnet` / `dotnet-hybrid`, and arbitrary unknown runtime profiles.
- The scripts must fail locally before touching fake `dotnet`, `cargo`, `winapp`, or `ISCC`; rust-only values still direct callers to rs portable, while unknown legacy spellings remain explicit-Hybrid-only instead of becoming retained `.NET` aliases.

## 2026-06-12: Forced rustup target add to the rs portable child-tool environment

- A packaging subagent found one remaining child-tool environment gap: `pack-rs-portable` and `build-rust-helpers` already forced child `cargo` invocations to `rust-only`, but the preceding optional `rustup target add` step still inherited the caller environment. No new dependency was needed; this is a fixed `std::process::Command` environment policy.
- `run_rustup_target_add_if_available(...)` now receives the same strict child-tool environment as cargo: `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, and `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_AI_REQUIRE_WINRT_BINDINGS=1`.
- The fake release-contract tooling can now record rustup separately from cargo. Added `rustup_target_add_is_forced_to_rust_only_runtime_profile`, proving a parent `hybrid` shell does not leak into target installation before the first rs portable helper builds.

Validation:

## 2026-06-12: Validated managed CJK font cache entries before marking them available

- Rechecked third-party options before changing the cache predicate. The app already depends on `ttf-parser`, which is the right lightweight parser for validating TrueType/OpenType bytes here; `fontdb` would help discover arbitrary system fonts, which this Rust-only portable boundary intentionally avoids. No new dependency was added.
- `font_download::cached_font_path_for_directory(...)`, `has_any_cjk_font_for_directory(...)`, and `ensure_font_for_directory(...)` now require a known NotoSans CJK asset file to parse as a font and expose at least one CJK glyph before it counts as cached/available.
- Settings status tests now install a real CJK font fixture for the Available case and cover the invalid known-name case as Not downloaded. Resource download tests also cover invalid downloaded font rejection, so empty or corrupt `Fonts/NotoSans*.ttf` files no longer make LongDoc PDF overlay or settings status look ready.

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app`

## 2026-06-12: Restricted explicit LongDoc PDF overlay fonts to managed cache assets

- Rechecked reusable font crates before changing the policy. `ttf-parser` is a strong parser for validating TrueType/OpenType internals, and `fontdb` is useful for font discovery and CSS-like matching, but this slice is a source-boundary guard rather than arbitrary font selection. No new dependency was added.
- `native_pdf_overlay_font_path(...)` now rejects explicit `SettingsSnapshot.cjk_font_path` values unless the path is a readable file, its file name matches the existing `font_download::font_assets()` NotoSans CJK allowlist, and its parent directory is the active Rust-managed `Fonts` cache (`SettingsSnapshot.cache_dir/Fonts`, or the default Easydict cache).
- Existing PDF overlay success tests now install the test font fixture into `Fonts/NotoSansSC-Regular.ttf` first. Added explicit regressions for unmanaged explicit paths and managed explicit paths, so stale package fonts or system font paths cannot satisfy the first rs portable LongDoc PDF overlay route by accident.

## 2026-06-12: Rejected linked entries in Rust zip-directory packaging

- A packaging subagent identified that the generic `easydict_packager zip-directory` helper still used `fs::metadata()`, which follows directory links. This helper is used for legacy/hybrid ZIP creation, so it should not be able to pull external `dotnet/`, `workers/`, or runtime files through symlink or Windows reparse-point entries.
- `zip-directory` now checks `DirEntry::file_type()` plus Windows `symlink_metadata().file_attributes()` before recursion and returns `ZipDirectoryError::UnsupportedDirectoryEntry` for symlink/reparse entries instead of following them.
- The link policy is shared with the existing rs portable directory validator flag helper, keeping portable ZIP validation and generic ZIP creation aligned.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_packager --check`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager zip_directory -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_packager --all-targets`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_packager --all-targets --features hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging`

## 2026-06-12: Normalized persisted LocalAI provider aliases at settings storage

- Rechecked the LocalAI provider alias requirement. No new dependency was needed: the existing `protocol_core::normalize_local_ai_provider_mode(...)` table already owns the canonical mapping used by Quick Translate, LongDoc, CLI env overrides, and Foundry config checks.
- Settings load/save now reuses that table for direct `LocalAIProvider` values. Legacy spellings such as `windows-ai`, `phi_silica`, `foundry-local`, `local-ai`, and `open_vino` load as canonical `WindowsAI`, `FoundryLocal`, or `OpenVINO`; unknown values normalize to `Auto`.
- `load_settings_file` now persists the cleanup back to disk, so first-release rs portable settings do not keep old worker-era provider spelling that could later diverge from native route decisions.

Validation:

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app --check`

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test settings_storage_behavior -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Rejected linked runtime roots during MSIX input preparation

- Rechecked whether this needed a directory-walking or junction crate. The existing Rust packager already uses standard-library symlink detection plus Windows `symlink_metadata().file_attributes()` for reparse points, so `easydict_msix_validate` now reuses the same pattern without adding a dependency.
- `prepare-package-inputs` now records unsupported linked/reparse entries by their own relative path before recursion. Under the Rust-only default profile, a `dotnet` or `workers` root that is a symlink or Windows reparse point is rejected before any prepared manifest is written.
- Added a real linked-`dotnet` regression plus a pure policy test so stale retained runtime payloads cannot be smuggled into the MSIX input tree through filesystem indirection.

Validation:

## 2026-06-12: Scanned Rust-only MSIX helper executables for .NET host markers

- Rechecked whether this should use a PE parser. `object`/`goblin` are plausible OSS PE/COFF parsers, but neither is currently in the workspace and the first rs portable validator already uses a focused marker strategy for renamed apphost protection. Reused that strategy here to keep MSIX and portable Rust-only rules aligned without adding a parser dependency.
- `easydict_msix_validate` now records required Rust helper executables whose contents contain retained .NET host/runtime markers (`hostfxr.dll`, `hostpolicy.dll`, `coreclr.dll`, `clrjit.dll`, `singlefilehost.exe`, CoreLib/runtimeconfig/deps, CompatHost/Workers) while indexing package payloads.
- Rust-only MSIX validation and nested bundle validation now reject allowlisted helper names if their bytes look like a renamed .NET apphost, closing the gap where path/name allowlists alone were insufficient.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_msix_validate rust_only_package -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Routed app LongDoc Retry Failed through Rust sidecar backend

- Parallel review confirmed the clean UI/state retry affordance still needs a later `LongDocumentState` field, but the backend can move now without touching `ui.rs`, `state.rs`, or WinFluent. No new library was needed: the existing Rust-native result JSON sidecar and retry backend already own the core behavior.
- Native-route file requests built by the app now derive a stable `*.result.json` sidecar path next to the translated output. Inline text without a stable output folder still leaves `resultJsonPath` unset.
- `Message::RetryLongDocument` now uses a separate start path and calls `retry_failed_native_text_long_document_from_result_json(...)` instead of reusing the full translate task. Missing sidecars fail locally before any provider or retained-worker lookup.

## 2026-06-12: Split hybrid release assets away from rs portable artifacts

- A packaging subagent found a release-flavor boundary leak: `release_flavor=hybrid` still depended on the rs portable job and uploaded `rs-portable/*.zip` beside hybrid/MSIX assets. No new third-party library was needed; this is a GitHub Actions contract fix.
- `publish-rs-portable` is now positively gated to `release_flavor == 'rs-portable'`, while `create-bundle` depends only on `prepare` and `publish-msix`. Hybrid releases no longer download `easydict-rs-portable-*` artifacts or upload rs portable ZIPs.
- Tightened the default Foundry Local CLI override denylist so retained host/runtime commands such as `hostpolicy.dll`, `coreclr.dll`, `clrjit.dll`, and `singlefilehost.exe` are rejected before any process spawn.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior release_workflow -- --nocapture`

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/easydict-foundry-local/Cargo.toml cli_executable_override_rejects_retained_dotnet_runtime_commands -- --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Made LongDoc Retry Failed sidecar-only at CLI/shim boundary

- Parallel audits found two small default-rs release gaps. No new third-party library was needed: both fixes are local CLI/shim/release-contract boundaries around existing Rust sidecar and packager code.
- `easydict_long_doc --retry-failed --result-json <sidecar>` no longer requires `--input` or `--target-language` before reading the sidecar. The initial request uses a placeholder shell only; the retry backend restores input/output mode, service, languages, output path, page range, and PDF route metadata from the checkpoint before export.
- `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1 -RetryFailed -ResultJsonPath <file>` now follows the same sidecar-only contract and still forces child helper/Cargo invocations to `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`.
- Strengthened rs portable validation/release coverage: allowlisted executable content scanning now includes CoreCLR host/runtime markers (`hostpolicy.dll`, `coreclr.dll`, `clrjit.dll`, `singlefilehost.exe`), and the extracted ZIP smoke now runs the public `Easydict.Rust.exe` entrypoint, not only `easydict_cli.exe`, while guarding against `dotnet.exe` and PowerShell launches.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_cli_behavior retry_failed -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior translate_long_doc_script -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior pack_rs_portable_zip_extracts_to -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager validate_rs_portable_rejects_allowlisted_exe_that_contains_dotnet_host_markers -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app --package easydict_packager --check`

## 2026-06-12: Hardened LongDoc retry sidecars and rs portable executable contents

- Rechecked the MDD request first. The Rust route is already covered through `lib/rs-mdict` MDD parsing/resource lookup, companion discovery, rich HTML data-URL inlining, Quick Translate `rawHtml` gating, and legacy settings MDD discovery; no new parser dependency was needed. The local MIT `rs-mdict` fork remains the best fit for this core path.
- Parallel audits then pointed at two small Rust-only boundary gaps. LongDoc Retry Failed sidecars now reject semantically incomplete checkpoints where a source chunk has neither translated text nor a failed marker, before calling any provider or rewriting output/sidecar files.
- `validate-rs-portable` now scans allowlisted executable payloads for obvious .NET host/runtime/assembly markers, so a renamed .NET apphost cannot slip through merely by using `Easydict.Rust.exe` or Rust helper executable names. This is intentionally marker-based for the first rs portable release; a deeper PE/CLR parser can be considered later if needed.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_behavior native_text_result_json_retry_failed_rejects_incomplete_checkpoint_without_export -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager validate_rs_portable_rejects_allowlisted_exe_that_contains_dotnet_host_markers -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml --package easydict_app --package easydict_packager --check`

## 2026-06-12: Guarded PDF overlay font fallback against unmanaged cache files

- Rechecked the next PDF/LongDoc Rust-only boundary after the Foundry SDK audit. No new crate was needed: `font_download` already owns the managed NotoSans CJK asset list, and LongDoc PDF overlay consumes that same cache helper.
- Added `font_download_cache_ignores_unmanaged_font_files`, proving random `Fonts/*.ttf` and temporary NotoSans-like files do not make the CJK font cache available.
- Added `native_pdf_overlay_font_fallback_rejects_unmanaged_cache_fonts`, proving LongDoc PDF overlay fallback itself fails through the managed font-cache boundary instead of scanning arbitrary cached font files.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test resource_download_behavior font_download_cache_ignores_unmanaged_font_files -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app long_document::tests::native_pdf_overlay_font_fallback_rejects_unmanaged_cache_fonts --lib -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app long_document::tests::native_pdf_overlay_font_fallback_uses_settings_cache_dir --lib -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Closed LocalAI CLI stream/grammar app-dir stale-payload gaps

- A read-only CLI audit confirmed the remaining easy LocalAI no-runtime gap was `windows-local-ai` with `stream --app-dir` and `grammar --app-dir`; `translate --app-dir`, `batch --app-dir`, and `stream --host` were already covered.
- Added stale-payload regressions for both modes using an app dir that contains `workers/localai`, `Easydict.CompatHost.exe`, `dotnet/dotnet.exe`, `hostfxr.dll`, and CoreLib markers.
- Both tests prove `--app-dir` remains a legacy no-op for rs CLI routing: the commands fail before emitting stdout, require a Rust-native LocalAI route, and do not mention retained worker, CompatHost, dotnet, or hostfxr payloads.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test cli_translate_behavior local_ai_stream_cli_app_dir_no_longer_enables_retained_worker_fallback -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test cli_translate_behavior local_ai_grammar_cli_app_dir_no_longer_enables_retained_worker_fallback -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Covered LongDoc LocalAI native route matrix before worker fallback

- Parallel subagent review pointed at LongDoc LocalAI routing as the next core guard after MDD and runtime-profile work. A separate library audit did not find a higher-quality replacement dependency for this slice; the existing Rust-native WindowsAI, Foundry Local, OpenVINO/NLLB, and `rs-mdict` libraries remain the right ownership boundaries.
- Added `local_ai_long_document_route_matrix_stays_native_before_worker_fallback`, which runs LongDoc LocalAI with stale `workers/longdoc`, `workers/localai`, `Easydict.CompatHost.exe`, `dotnet/dotnet.exe`, `hostfxr.dll`, and CoreLib markers in `app_dir`.
- The matrix covers explicit WindowsAI, Auto with ready WindowsAI, Auto falling through to native Foundry probing, explicit FoundryLocal configured endpoint, and OpenVINO cache preflight. Each path either succeeds through an injected Rust-native client or fails on a native/preflight boundary without retained worker, CompatHost, or dotnet wording.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_behavior local_ai_long_document_route_matrix_stays_native_before_worker_fallback -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Forced LongDoc PowerShell shim children to Rust-only profile

- A read-only subagent audit found no remaining executable dotnet/CompatHost/worker launch path in `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1`; the remaining risk was inherited parent environment such as `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid`.
- `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1` now wraps both packaged helper and `-UseCargo` invocations with temporary `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, then restores the caller environment in `finally`.
- The retired `-UseDotnetLegacy` compatibility switch is hidden from normal PowerShell help/completion while still failing locally before any helper starts. The rs README now documents only the Rust helper/Cargo development modes and clarifies that `-AppDir` only locates `easydict_long_doc.exe`.
- `release_contract_behavior` now runs fake helper/cargo paths with parent profiles set to `hybrid` and proves the child process receives `rust-only`.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo fmt -p easydict_packager -- --check`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior translate_long_doc -- --nocapture`
- PowerShell parser check for `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1`
- `git diff --check -- scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1 rs/README.md migration-list.md refactor-progress.md rs/crates/easydict_packager/tests/release_contract_behavior.rs`

## 2026-06-12: Aligned Makefile MSIX validators with Rust-only default profile

- A read-only subagent audit found `validate-msix` already omitted `--runtime-profile` when `RUNTIME_PROFILE` was empty, while `msix-x64`, `msix-x86`, and `msix-arm64` still passed `--runtime-profile "$(RUNTIME_PROFILE)"` to the Rust validator.
- The three MSIX package targets now normalize `RUNTIME_PROFILE` and pass `--runtime-profile "$runtime_profile"` only when the caller provided a value. When it is empty, they call `easydict_msix_validate -- <msix> --allow-unsigned`, preserving the validator's Rust-only default.
- Extended `release_contract_behavior` so the Makefile contract covers the post-package validators as well as `validate-msix`.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo fmt -p easydict_packager -- --check`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior legacy_dotnet_packaging_paths_reject_rust_only_and_require_hybrid_profile -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Hardened LongDoc CLI app-dir no-op against stale payloads

- Rechecked the LongDoc native output preflight first; the multi-output and result JSON sidecar failure boundary was already implemented and covered, so this slice focused on the still-useful CLI app-dir runtime guard.
- `app_dir_ignores_stale_dotnet_payload_markers_and_does_not_enable_worker_lookup` now builds an app dir containing stale `workers/longdoc`, `workers/localai`, `Easydict.CompatHost.exe`, `dotnet/dotnet.exe`, `hostfxr.dll`, and CoreLib markers. The default `easydict_long_doc --service windows-local-ai --app-dir` path still fails on the Rust-native LocalAI boundary without worker/CompatHost/dotnet wording.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test long_document_cli_behavior app_dir_ignores_stale_dotnet_payload_markers_and_does_not_enable_worker_lookup -- --exact --nocapture`

## 2026-06-12: Validated sign-and-install before local MSIX install

- A parallel worker handled this packaging slice. No new dependency was needed; the existing Rust `easydict_msix_validate` CLI already owns MSIX payload policy.
- `dotnet/scripts/sign-and-install.ps1` accepts `-RuntimeProfile` and now runs `cargo run --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml -p easydict_msix_validate -- <PackagePath> --allow-unsigned` before signing, uninstalling, or calling `Add-AppxPackage`. Empty/default/rust-only profiles let the validator use its Rust-only default; explicit `Hybrid` is the only case that forwards `--runtime-profile hybrid`.
- Added fake-tool release-contract coverage for the default Rust-only validator path and the explicit Hybrid forwarding path.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo fmt -p easydict_packager -- --check`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior sign_and_install -- --nocapture`
- `git diff --check -- dotnet/scripts/sign-and-install.ps1 rs/crates/easydict_packager/tests/release_contract_behavior.rs`

## 2026-06-12: Restricted retained-worker hybrid opt-in to Easydict env

- Rechecked the retained runtime-profile boundary after a read-only subagent audit. No new dependency was needed; this is a small Rust policy change.
- `RuntimeRoutePolicy` now treats `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` as the only environment opt-in that can enable retained workers when the compatibility feature is compiled. Generic `RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` no longer enables retained workers by itself; `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` and unknown generic values still participate as kill switches.
- Updated the retained-feature runtime-policy test to cover `generic_hybrid_profile_does_not_enable_retained_workers_when_feature_is_compiled`.

Validation:

- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --lib runtime_policy -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --lib --features retained-dotnet-workers runtime_policy -- --nocapture`

Note:

- An earlier broad `cargo test -p easydict_app runtime_policy` attempt compiled unrelated integration tests and failed with disk/PDB/memory limits (`no space on device`, `LNK1318`, `LNK1140`), so the focused `--lib` commands above were used for this policy slice.

## 2026-06-12: Aligned persistent MDX suggestions with MDict header normalization

- Rechecked the local dictionary index/header-normalization gap before changing code. No new dependency was needed; the app now reuses the existing `lib/rs-mdict` normalization helper instead of duplicating MDict strip/case rules.
- `LexIndex` keeps the compatible LXDX binary format while allowing callers to provide pre-normalized trie keys. `LocalDictionaryIndexService` records a header-aware manifest normalization id and normalizes queries with the matching `StripKey` / `KeyCaseSensitive` rules when the MDX header is readable.
- Added coverage for pre-normalized LexIndex entries and for persistent MDX indexes whose headers set `StripKey=Yes` or `KeyCaseSensitive=Yes`.

Validation:

- `cargo test --manifest-path lib/rs-mdict/Cargo.toml --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test lex_index_behavior -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test local_dictionary_index_behavior -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo test -p easydict_app --test quick_translate_behavior local_dictionary_suggestions -- --nocapture`
- `cd rs; cargo check -p easydict_app --all-targets`

## 2026-06-12: Treated direct WinUI old runtime-profile aliases as Rust-only

- Rechecked the WinUI MSBuild runtime-profile boundary before changing code. No new dependency was needed: this is an MSBuild property contract.
- `Easydict.WinUI.csproj` now treats only `RuntimeProfile=Hybrid` / `hybrid` as the retained `.NET` worker opt-in. Empty, Rust-only aliases, old `dotnet` / `dotnet-hybrid` spellings, and unknown values all resolve to Rust-only behavior with `BuildWorkerOutputs=false` and `EnableInProcLongDocFallback=false`.
- Updated `WorkerPackagingTests` contract text so old dotnet aliases cannot be reintroduced as hybrid opt-ins.

Validation:

- `cd dotnet; dotnet msbuild src/Easydict.WinUI/Easydict.WinUI.csproj -nologo -p:RuntimeProfile=dotnet -p:Configuration=Debug -getProperty:IsHybridRuntimeProfile -getProperty:IsRustOnlyRuntimeProfile -getProperty:BuildWorkerOutputs -getProperty:EnableInProcLongDocFallback`
- `cd dotnet; dotnet msbuild src/Easydict.WinUI/Easydict.WinUI.csproj -nologo -p:RuntimeProfile=dotnet-hybrid -p:Configuration=Debug -getProperty:IsHybridRuntimeProfile -getProperty:IsRustOnlyRuntimeProfile -getProperty:BuildWorkerOutputs -getProperty:EnableInProcLongDocFallback`
- `cd dotnet; dotnet msbuild src/Easydict.WinUI/Easydict.WinUI.csproj -nologo -p:RuntimeProfile=banana -p:Configuration=Debug -getProperty:IsHybridRuntimeProfile -getProperty:IsRustOnlyRuntimeProfile -getProperty:BuildWorkerOutputs -getProperty:EnableInProcLongDocFallback`
- `cd dotnet; dotnet msbuild src/Easydict.WinUI/Easydict.WinUI.csproj -nologo -p:RuntimeProfile=rust-only -p:Configuration=Debug -getProperty:IsHybridRuntimeProfile -getProperty:IsRustOnlyRuntimeProfile -getProperty:BuildWorkerOutputs -getProperty:EnableInProcLongDocFallback`
- `cd dotnet; dotnet msbuild src/Easydict.WinUI/Easydict.WinUI.csproj -nologo -p:RuntimeProfile=Hybrid -p:Configuration=Debug -getProperty:IsHybridRuntimeProfile -getProperty:IsRustOnlyRuntimeProfile -getProperty:BuildWorkerOutputs -getProperty:EnableInProcLongDocFallback`

Note: a focused `dotnet test tests/Easydict.WinUI.Tests/Easydict.WinUI.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RustOnlyWinUIBuild_DisablesRetainedWorkersAndInProcLongDocFallback --no-restore` was retried after a short wait, but the test assembly currently fails to compile on unrelated existing LongDoc/layout test surfaces (`DocLayoutYoloService`, `LayoutDetectionStrategy`, `VisionLayoutDetectionService`, `TableStructureRecognitionService`, and `DocumentOutputMode` ambiguity) before this static contract test can run.

## 2026-06-12: Pinned OCR current-app-dir runner to Rust-native Custom API

- Rechecked the OCR migration surface before changing code. No new dependency was needed: the existing Rust-native OCR provider stack already owns Windows OCR, Ollama, and Custom API HTTP routes, and the old OCR worker is retired.
- Added `current_app_dir_runner_uses_custom_api_provider_despite_hybrid_runtime_profile`, covering the production helper used by the app-side OCR task. Even if the parent process sets both `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid`, the current-app-dir runner still sends a native Custom API HTTP request and does not expose legacy OCR worker or CompatHost markers.
