- 2026-06-08: Synchronized MSIX rust-only payload validation with the portable no-runtime policy.
  - No new third-party crate was added; `easydict_msix_validate` already owns Easydict-specific MSIX payload rules over the existing `zip`/`quick_xml` parser surface.
  - The rust-only profile now rejects nested `host/fxr`, `shared/Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App`, and `shared/Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` runtime layouts, plus loose .NET runtime/framework files such as `System.*.dll`, `Microsoft.CSharp*`, `Microsoft.Win32*`, `WindowsBase.dll`, `PresentationCore.dll`, `netstandard.dll`, `dotnet.exe`, `createdump.exe`, and old C# `Easydict.NativeBridge*` / `Easydict.SidecarClient*` payloads.
  - Full `easydict_msix_validate` test coverage now exercises these additional rust-only rejects without breaking the explicit hybrid retained-worker package gate.
- 2026-06-08: Synchronized Rust portable payload validation with the same no-runtime marker list.
  - No new third-party crate was added; `easydict_packager` already validates both directories and ZIPs with the existing Rust `zip` implementation.
  - `validate-rs-portable` now rejects the same loose .NET runtime/framework residue added to the MSIX rust-only profile, including `dotnet.exe`, `createdump.exe`, `netstandard.dll`, `WindowsBase.dll`, `PresentationCore.dll`, `Microsoft.CSharp*`, `Microsoft.Win32*`, and nested `shared/Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` layouts.
  - Extended the existing directory and ZIP portable validation tests and reran the full packager suite.
- 2026-06-08: Rejected retained worker shared/projection DLLs in rs-only package validators.
  - No library research was needed; this is another product-specific payload policy update over the existing ZIP/MSIX entry readers.
  - Both `validate-rs-portable` and `easydict_msix_validate --runtime-profile rust-only` now reject loose retained-worker shared DLLs such as `Microsoft.Windows.SDK.NET.dll`, `WinRT.Runtime.dll`, `Microsoft.Windows.UI.Xaml.dll`, `Microsoft.WinUI.dll`, `Microsoft.InteractiveExperiences.Projection.dll`, and `Microsoft.Web.WebView2.Core.Projection.dll` even when they are copied outside the `workers/` directory.
  - Extended portable directory/ZIP tests and MSIX rust-only loose-payload tests, then reran the full packager and MSIX validator suites.
- 2026-06-08: Aligned MSIX rust-only rejects with the portable no-C#-payload policy.
  - No new third-party crate was needed; this is package payload policy over the existing Rust MSIX ZIP reader.
  - `easydict_msix_validate --runtime-profile rust-only` now rejects loose legacy C# business assemblies and app binaries that the portable validator already rejects, including `Easydict.WinUI*`, `Easydict.TranslationService.dll`, `Easydict.DocumentExport.dll`, `Easydict.Llm.Streaming.dll`, `Easydict.OpenVINO.dll`, `Easydict.WindowsAI.dll`, `LexIndex.dll`, `MDict.Csharp.dll`, and `Polyglot.TextLayout.dll`.
  - Extended the MSIX rust-only loose-payload test so these assemblies cannot slip into a rust-only MSIX path if future scripts accidentally stage them.
- 2026-06-08: Made the MSIX validator default to Rust-only payload policy.
  - No new library was needed; this is another runtime-profile default/packaging-policy adjustment in the existing Rust validator.
  - `MsixValidationOptions::default()` now uses `PackageRuntimeProfile::RustOnly`, so direct library callers and CLI runs that omit `--runtime-profile` reject retained workers, `.NET` runtime layouts, and legacy C# payloads by default.
  - Hybrid/coexistence validation remains available only when callers explicitly pass `--runtime-profile hybrid` or construct options with `PackageRuntimeProfile::Hybrid`; Makefile, release workflow, and package-and-install paths still pass an explicit profile for dotnet packages.
  - Updated the README smoke example to pass `--runtime-profile hybrid` for retained MSIX packages, and made `package-and-install.ps1` pass the already validated `RuntimeProfile` value into the Rust validator instead of keeping a separate hard-coded validator default.
  - Added unit coverage for the default profile and converted hybrid package-layout tests to use explicit hybrid options so the tests no longer depend on an unsafe default.
- 2026-06-08: Made retained-worker runtime-profile environment parsing conservative across both profile variables.
  - No new third-party crate was needed; this is runtime policy gating over the existing explicit `retained-dotnet-workers` compatibility feature.
  - `RetainedWorkerPolicy::from_environment()` now considers both `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE` and `RUNTIME_PROFILE` when the retained-worker feature is compiled. Any rust-only or unknown profile source disables retained LocalAI/LongDoc workers before a conflicting `hybrid` source can enable them.
  - The default no-feature build still ignores both environment variables and returns `all_disabled()`, so rs portable-facing Quick Translate/CLI/LongDoc routes cannot be reopened by shell or workflow runtime-profile values.
  - Updated rs README verification commands from the retired `compat_protocol` test target to `protocol_behavior`.
- 2026-06-08: Tightened rs portable validation from blocklist-only to first-release allowlist.
  - No library research was needed; this is packaging policy over the existing Rust directory/ZIP entry readers.
  - `validate-rs-portable` still reports known retained `.NET` payload entries first for clear diagnostics, then requires the package to contain only the first-release portable files. The original allowlist also accepted the legacy `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` alias; the later 2026-06-09 portable-alias cleanup removed that alias from first-release rs portable packages.
  - Added coverage for missing required entries and unknown extra payload such as a stray DLL that does not match the retained `.NET` blocklist.
- 2026-06-08: Moved `.NET` runtime extraction profile guard into the Rust packager library API.
  - No new third-party crate was needed; this is a packaging-policy guard around the existing `reqwest` download and ZIP extraction path.
  - `ExtractDotnetRuntimeOptions` now carries an explicit `PackageRuntimeProfile`, and `download_and_extract_dotnet_runtime(...)` rejects anything other than `Hybrid` before RID validation, temp archive creation, HTTP download, or output extraction.
  - The same library-level guard rejects `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`, and unknown profile values before network access, so direct library callers cannot bypass the CLI's `extract-dotnet-runtime --runtime-profile hybrid` check.
- 2026-06-08: Made release-publish default to rs portable-only release assets.
  - No third-party library applies; this is GitHub Actions release policy around existing Rust and legacy dotnet artifact jobs.
  - Added a `release_flavor` workflow input defaulting to `rs-portable`. Tag pushes and manual default runs now skip the dotnet/MSIX/installer bundle path and use a dedicated `create-rs-portable-release` job that downloads only `easydict-rs-portable-*` artifacts and uploads only those ZIPs to the GitHub Release.
  - Kept the legacy dotnet/MSIX/installer/WinGet path available only behind explicit `release_flavor=hybrid`; `publish-msix`, `create-bundle`, and `publish-winget` are all gated on that flavor.
- 2026-06-08: Added neutral default app-dir aliases for Rust-only runners.
  - No library research was needed; this is an API-surface cleanup after default routes were already fixed to Rust-only policy and no longer probe retained worker paths.
  - Added `with_app_dir` aliases for Quick Translate service execution, local dictionary suggestions, LongDoc, and OCR so first rs portable-facing code can use neutral names.
  - Updated representative default behavior tests to call the new aliases and renamed the LongDoc default-rs test away from packaged-worker wording. The default `protocol` facade rustdoc now describes legacy compatibility IPC as optional instead of naming retained worker IPC in the default surface.
  - Re-exported the neutral aggregate `run_quick_translate_with_app_dir(...)` helper from the crate root and renamed private Quick Translate/LongDoc app-dir internals away from `packaged_app_dir`.
  - Removed the hidden `with_packaged_app_dir` default helper wrappers from the crate-root re-export surface and moved the default Quick Translate/local dictionary/LongDoc behavior tests to the neutral `with_app_dir` calls. Only explicit `retained-dotnet-workers` worker-policy compatibility helpers still carry the old packaged-app-dir wording.
- 2026-06-08: Moved browser Native Messaging registrar defaults to the rs coexistence root.
  - No new library was needed; this is a packaging/registration boundary cleanup for the existing Rust native messaging registrar.
  - `DEFAULT_BRIDGE_ROOT_NAME` now resolves to `EasydictRs`, so install/uninstall/status defaults stage manifests under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\EasydictRs\browser-bridge` instead of the dotnet legacy `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Easydict\browser-bridge`.
  - Added an explicit `LEGACY_BRIDGE_ROOT_NAME` / `legacy_bridge_directory(...)` helper for dotnet coexistence checks, while keeping custom `--bridge-root-name` support for manual override.
  - Updated browser registrar coverage so default parsing and `default_bridge_directory(...)` assert the rs portable location and legacy directory behavior remains explicit.
- 2026-06-08: Made legacy dotnet publish/MSIX entrypoints reject RustOnly before self-contained publish.
  - No third-party library applies; this is release-script policy around existing PowerShell, Makefile, and GitHub Actions entrypoints.
  - `dotnet/scripts/publish.ps1`, `dotnet/scripts/package-and-install.ps1`, `dotnet/scripts/Package-Msix.ps1`, and dotnet `Makefile` publish/MSIX targets now fail early when `RuntimeProfile` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE` normalizes to RustOnly, with guidance to use `rs/scripts/Package-Portable.ps1` for the first rs release.
  - Release and ARM64 MSIX smoke workflows no longer expose `rust-only` in their manual runtime-profile choices and also guard the dotnet/MSIX jobs against RustOnly aliases before restore/publish.
  - `Extract-DotnetRuntime.ps1` now only accepts explicit `Hybrid`/`hybrid` at the PowerShell layer, matching the retained-worker-only runtime extraction contract before invoking the Rust packager.
- 2026-06-08: Let native PDF table-cell text go through LongDoc translation.
  - No library research was needed; this is a formula-preservation policy fix over existing Rust PDF/TATR source-block metadata.
  - `SourceBlockType::TableCell` no longer makes a block opaque by itself. Text table cells now translate normally, while formula-like cells and numeric table cells still use the existing opaque preservation heuristics.
  - Updated PDF source extraction coverage so TATR table cells keep table export metadata without setting `translation_skipped` or `preserve_original_text_in_pdf_export` when the cell contains ordinary text.
- 2026-06-08: Added Quick CLI LocalAI environment overrides before retained-worker routing.
  - No third-party library was needed; this mirrors the existing LongDoc CLI env-override surface over the Rust-owned `SettingsSnapshot`.
  - `easydict_cli` now applies `EASYDICT_LOCAL_AI_PROVIDER` / `LOCAL_AI_PROVIDER`, Foundry Local endpoint/model env vars, OpenVINO device env vars, and `EASYDICT_OPENVINO_CACHE_DIR` / `EASYDICT_CACHE_DIR` after loading settings and before building Quick Translate LocalAI requests.
  - Quick CLI tests now cover env-forced OpenVINO cache preflight and env-forced Foundry native routing, and clear inherited LocalAI env override variables in existing worker-retirement regressions so ambient developer env cannot accidentally reopen retained-worker wording.
- 2026-06-08: Moved Built-in AI embedded secrets into the Rust app crate.
  - No third-party library was needed; this is resource ownership cleanup over the existing Rust AES-CBC Built-in AI secret decryptor.
  - `openai_compatible.rs` now embeds `rs/crates/easydict_app/resources/EncryptedSecrets.json` instead of compiling from `dotnet/src/Easydict.TranslationService/Resources/EncryptedSecrets.json`, reducing the default rs provider path's dependency on the dotnet source tree.
  - Built-in AI proxy/device-registration tests still prove the embedded endpoint/key decrypt and request planning work after the resource move.
- 2026-06-08: Covered Quick CLI stream/batch LocalAI legacy-option no-op behavior.
  - A read-only subagent audit found `translate` had strong `--app-dir`/legacy `--host` LocalAI worker-retirement coverage, while `stream` and `batch` only had generic retired-route coverage.
  - Added CLI behavior regressions proving `stream --service windows-local-ai --host ...` and `batch --service windows-local-ai --app-dir ...` still fail locally with Rust-native-route wording in a hybrid environment and do not probe retained LocalAI worker paths or mention CompatHost.
- 2026-06-08: Hardened default GUI streaming LocalAI against retained-worker re-entry.
  - A read-only subagent audit confirmed the current streaming path already ended at `RetainedWorkerPolicy::all_disabled()` through `local_ai_quick_translate_local_error(...)`, but recommended making the policy explicit like the non-streaming path.
  - No new third-party library was needed. `run_quick_translate_streaming_service_with_app_dir(...)` now delegates through an internal worker-policy + Foundry-resolver helper, defaulting to `RetainedWorkerPolicy::all_disabled()` before the retained LocalAI bridge branch can be considered.
  - Added a hidden neutral streaming app-dir test helper with injected Foundry resolver, then covered Auto LocalAI `TranslateStream` in a hybrid environment where Foundry returns no endpoint and OpenVINO cache is missing. The regression proves the result is a local Rust-native-route error, no stream chunk is emitted, and no `.NET`/LocalAI worker/CompatHost wording appears.
- 2026-06-08: Moved the Foundry Local control-plane wrapper into `lib/`.
  - Researched the reusable library option first. Microsoft now publishes an official Rust `foundry-local-sdk`, documented with WinML support and native catalog/model/chat APIs, and the Microsoft Foundry Local repository advertises native SDKs for C#, JavaScript, Python, and Rust (`https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry-local/reference/reference-sdk-current`, `https://github.com/microsoft/Foundry-Local`). This slice keeps the existing CLI-backed behavior stable and leaves an SDK-backed adapter as the next incremental swap inside the new lib.
  - Added `lib/easydict-foundry-local` to own Foundry Local endpoint discovery, runtime status parsing, start/load prepare flow, endpoint normalization, `/models` endpoint derivation, model alias/device preference resolution, and Foundry-specific recoverable error codes.
  - `easydict_app` now delegates status/prepare helpers to the lib and keeps only app-owned settings persistence, OpenAI-compatible request planning, route decisions, and proxy bypass checks. The public app re-exports continue to support existing Foundry behavior tests.
  - Verified the default and `retained-dotnet-workers` feature builds so this boundary cleanup does not reopen LocalAI retained-worker paths.
- 2026-06-08: Added the first Foundry Local SDK-backed adapter boundary under `lib/`.
  - Rechecked the reusable SDK option before adding the boundary. Microsoft Learn documents `foundry-local-sdk` for Rust with `FoundryLocalManager`, EP registration, catalog lookup, model download/load, and native chat APIs; crates.io currently reports `foundry-local-sdk` 1.2.1 as MIT with optional `winml` support. Because the SDK build can bundle Foundry Local Core/ONNX Runtime native artifacts, the default rs portable path still does not enable it directly.
  - Added `FoundryLocalSdkModel`, `FoundryLocalSdkModelProvider`, `FoundryLocalSdkPrepareOutcome`, and `prepare_foundry_local_sdk_model(...)` to `lib/easydict-foundry-local`. The helper fixes the future SDK adapter contract around execution-provider registration, model alias resolution, cached vs uncached model handling, download-before-load ordering, missing alias reporting, and invalid SDK model-id errors.
  - Added lib-level mock coverage for the SDK prepare flow without adding a default `foundry-local-sdk` dependency or changing current CLI-backed Foundry routes. This gives the next SDK-backed provider slice a tested `lib/` boundary while keeping app modules free of large third-party SDK wrapping code.
- 2026-06-08: Added the actual feature-gated official Foundry Local SDK provider.
  - `lib/easydict-foundry-local` now has an empty default feature set plus optional `sdk` / `sdk-winml` features. The `sdk` feature depends on `foundry-local-sdk = 1.2.1` and a local Tokio runtime; `sdk-winml` enables the SDK's WinML feature. The rs portable default path still does not enable either feature, so the SDK build script and native Foundry Local Core/ONNX Runtime payload are not pulled into the first portable package.
  - Added `FoundryLocalSdkProvider`, which wraps `FoundryLocalManager::create(...)`, `download_and_register_eps(None)`, `catalog().get_model(...)`, `is_cached()`, `get_model_variant(...)`, `download(...)`, and `load()`, then maps SDK errors back into Easydict's Foundry error codes. The app crate still sees only the lib-owned `FoundryLocalSdkModelProvider` trait.
  - Hardened `prepare_foundry_local_sdk_model(...)` so failed EP registration returns a local not-ready outcome before alias lookup/download/load. Added mock coverage for that branch and compiled the optional `sdk` feature to verify the real adapter against the official crate API.
- 2026-06-08: Started the Rust WindowsAI/Phi Silica control-plane boundary.
  - Rechecked reusable options before replacing the `.NET Easydict.WindowsAI` surface. Phi Silica is a Windows App SDK Limited Access Feature and Microsoft documents `LanguageModel.GetReadyState()`, `EnsureReadyAsync()`, `CreateAsync()`, and generation APIs under `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text`; the production Rust binding route should use Microsoft `windows` / `windows-bindgen` with the Windows App SDK AI WinMD rather than the unstable third-party `rs_ai_phi_silica` crate (`https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/ai/apis/phi-silica`, `https://github.com/microsoft/windows-rs`, `https://docs.rs/windows-bindgen/latest/windows_bindgen/`).
  - Added `lib/easydict-windows-ai` with the Phi Silica ready-state enum, `.NET` resource-key/status mapping, Windows AI baseline + UBR check, diagnostic marker detection, a `WindowsAiLanguageModelProbe` trait, and an unsupported default probe. This moves the status/probe semantics out of `easydict_app` and gives the later WinRT client a focused home under `lib/`.
  - Quick Translate LocalAI route decisions now distinguish `ProbeWindowsAi` from Foundry/OpenVINO/retained-worker paths. Default Auto dispatch probes WindowsAI first, then continues to Foundry Local and OpenVINO; explicit WindowsAI now fails with Rust-native Phi Silica generation-route wording instead of being treated as a retained-worker compatibility route, even under the explicit `retained-dotnet-workers` feature.
  - LongDoc native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF routing now also performs the WindowsAI probe before Foundry/OpenVINO fallback when building its native quick-translate probe request.
  - Still remaining for this slice: the real WinRT `LanguageModel` prepare/generate/stream client.
- 2026-06-08: Finished WindowsAI settings status and prepare-task plumbing in Rust.
  - Rechecked the replacement route and kept the official Microsoft `windows` / `windows-bindgen` + Windows App SDK AI WinMD path as the intended production binding route; no third-party wrapper was added for this control-plane step.
  - Extended `lib/easydict-windows-ai` with `prepare_windows_ai_model(...)`, `WindowsAiError`, and probe-driven `ensure_ready(...)` behavior. The lib now covers already-ready, not-ready-then-ready, not-ready-still-not-ready, and incompatible states without app-owned Phi status branching.
  - Settings runtime status now carries a WindowsAI status field and exposes an injected-probe path so a future real WinRT probe can replace the current unsupported default without changing app state handling.
  - `PrepareLocalAiModel` now routes FoundryLocal to Rust-native Foundry prepare, Auto/WindowsAI to the Rust WindowsAI prepare task, and OpenVINO to the existing Download-model path. `WindowsAiPrepareFinished` writes the Phi status/progress back locally and does not start retained workers.
- 2026-06-08: Moved Phi Silica prompt/generation/error semantics into the WindowsAI Rust lib.
  - Rechecked the `.NET Easydict.WindowsAI` behavior before extending the Rust boundary: `PhiSilicaTranslationService` validates empty text and target Auto locally, never implicitly prepares on translation, uses deterministic generation options (`Temperature=0.1`, `TopK=1`, `TopP=0.9`), builds the shared translation/grammar prompts, cleans non-streaming `Translation:`/quoted output, filters empty stream chunks while preserving space tokens, and maps Phi response statuses to Easydict translation error kinds.
  - `lib/easydict-windows-ai` now owns the reusable Phi translation/grammar request types, generation options, response/status/error mapping, prompt builders, output cleanup, a testable `WindowsAiLanguageModelClient` trait, and `translate_with_client(...)` / `translate_stream_with_client(...)` / `correct_grammar_stream_with_client(...)` helpers.
  - Added `prepare_windows_ai_client(...)` so a real WinRT client can run the .NET-equivalent Ready/NotReady `EnsureReady -> WarmUp("Reply with OK.") -> Ready` path and map warmup failures to `WindowsLocalAI_Status_RuntimeUnhealthy` or unsupported-baseline diagnostics.
  - Cargo cannot include `../lib/easydict-windows-ai` as an `rs/Cargo.toml` workspace member because it is outside the workspace root, so the crate remains a path dependency of `easydict_app` and is verified directly with `--manifest-path`.
- 2026-06-08: Locked down the WindowsAI WinMD locator and bindgen contract before implementing the real WinRT client.
  - Rechecked the official route again: Microsoft documents Phi Silica under Windows App SDK AI `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text.LanguageModel`, using `GetReadyState()`, `EnsureReadyAsync()`, `CreateAsync()`, and `GenerateResponseAsync(...)`; `windows-bindgen` supports combining `--in default` with a custom WinMD metadata directory and namespace filters.
  - `lib/easydict-windows-ai` now requires the complete public Windows App SDK AI metadata set (`Microsoft.Windows.AI.winmd`, `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Foundation.winmd`, and `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text.winmd`) before reporting a usable WinMD set. The local NuGet cache contains the project-resolved `microsoft.windowsappsdk.ai\2.0.185\metadata` files, matching the `.NET Easydict.WindowsAI` package graph.
  - Added tests for explicit metadata env override, explicit NuGet root override, latest semantic package-version selection, missing Foundation metadata rejection, and the canonical `windows-bindgen` args (`--in default <metadata-dir> --out <bindings.rs> --filter Microsoft.Windows.AI Microsoft.Windows.AI.Foundation Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text`).
  - This was the pre-client step; the later default-client slice replaced the unsupported app default with the Rust WinRT `LanguageModel` client and a local unsupported fallback when WinRT is unavailable.
- 2026-06-08: Wired Quick Translate to an injectable Rust WindowsAI/Phi client route.
  - Rechecked the official route again before wiring execution: the intended production backend remains Microsoft Windows App SDK AI `Microsoft.Windows.AI.Text.LanguageModel` through `windows` / `windows-bindgen`; no third-party Phi wrapper was added.
  - Added Quick Translate entry points that accept a `WindowsAiLanguageModelClient` and run `lib/easydict-windows-ai` translation, streaming translation, and grammar correction helpers. Explicit WindowsAI now uses the injected Phi client without falling back to Foundry/OpenVINO/retained workers; Auto uses it only when the probe reports Ready, otherwise it continues the existing Foundry Local then OpenVINO fallback order.
  - The default packaged runner now uses the Rust-owned default WindowsAI client; unavailable WinMD/WinRT or `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_AI_DISABLE_WINRT=1` reports a local unsupported status instead of starting retained workers.
  - Added behavior coverage for explicit WindowsAI injected-client translation, streaming chunk delivery, grammar correction parsing, Auto Ready priority over Foundry, and Auto NotReady fallback continuation.
- 2026-06-08: Routed Rust-native LongDoc chunks through the WindowsAI/Phi client.
  - No new third-party dependency was needed. The LongDoc adapter reuses `lib/easydict-windows-ai` `translate_with_client(...)`, language mapping, prompts, cleanup, and response/error semantics, keeping the WinRT/Phi wrapper boundary under `lib/`.
  - Added `WindowsAiNativeLongDocumentTranslator` and a hidden injected-client LongDoc app-dir entrypoint for behavior tests. Explicit WindowsAI now translates native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF chunks through the client without falling back to Foundry/OpenVINO or retained LongDoc/LocalAI workers.
  - Auto LocalAI LongDoc now uses WindowsAI only when the client reports Ready; NotReady/unsupported continues the existing Foundry Local then OpenVINO fallback order.
  - Added `long_document_behavior` coverage for explicit WindowsAI native chunk translation, Auto Ready priority before Foundry probing, and Auto NotReady Foundry fallback continuation.
- 2026-06-08: Preserved LongDoc context-pass semantics on the WindowsAI route.
  - No new dependency was needed. The WindowsAI LongDoc adapter now detects native document-context map/reduce prompts and sends them through raw `LanguageModel` generation instead of wrapping them in the normal translation prompt.
  - Plain chunk translation still uses `lib/easydict-windows-ai` `translate_with_client(...)`, so language validation, target-Auto rejection, output cleanup, and Phi response/error mapping remain centralized in the lib.
  - Added `windows_ai_long_document_context_pass_uses_raw_generation_before_translation`, which proves the context JSON prompt is not wrapped as a translation task and that the generated summary/glossary feeds the subsequent translation prompt.
- 2026-06-08: Removed the last Settings-status hardcoded WindowsAI unsupported probe from the default app helper.
  - No new library was needed. This slice reuses the Rust-owned `lib/easydict-windows-ai` default client that already wraps the official Windows App SDK AI WinRT route.
  - `settings_status::status_for_directory(...)` now creates `default_windows_ai_language_model_client()` just like the normal Settings loading path; unavailable WinRT or `EASYDICT_WINDOWS_AI_DISABLE_WINRT=1` still returns local unsupported through the lib, not through a retained worker.
  - The deterministic filesystem-only status tests now use a fixed test status, while a new injected-probe test proves the directory-status core is no longer fixed to `UnsupportedWindowsAiProbe`.
- 2026-06-08: Added default WindowsAI health fingerprinting to the Rust Phi client.
  - Rechecked the small OS/registry access option before changing the lib: `windows-sys 0.61.2` is part of Microsoft's `windows-rs` project, is MIT/Apache-2.0, and was already present in the broader rs lockfile. The dependency stays inside `lib/easydict-windows-ai` instead of adding Win32 probing code to the app crate (`https://crates.io/crates/windows-sys/0.61.2`, `https://github.com/microsoft/windows-rs`).
  - `DefaultWindowsAiLanguageModelClient` now implements `health_fingerprint()` by reading the current Windows OS build plus `UBR`, inferring the Windows App SDK AI package version from the selected WinMD metadata directory, and reporting process/backend/component diagnostics. Non-Windows or unavailable OS data returns no fingerprint rather than creating a fake status.
  - Settings/runtime status and prepare/warmup failure mapping can now apply the existing unsupported-baseline logic through the default client, not only through injected tests, while still falling back locally when WinRT or metadata is unavailable.
- 2026-06-08: Removed the remaining default public `with_packaged_app_dir` aliases from app modules.
  - A read-only subagent audit found no default compiled path that can start `.NET` workers, but it did find hidden module-level legacy aliases whose names still implied packaged worker lookup.
  - Removed the no-worker-policy aliases from Quick Translate, LongDoc, local dictionary suggestions, and OCR. Default callers use the neutral `*_with_app_dir` helpers; packaged-app-dir worker-policy helpers remain available only behind `retained-dotnet-workers` for coexistence audits.
  - Added `default_api_boundary_behavior` to prevent these legacy default aliases from reappearing while preserving explicit retained-worker audit helper names under their feature gate.
- 2026-06-08: Split generic packager diagnostics out of the rs portable README command block.
  - No new dependency was needed. This is documentation and regression-test hardening around the existing Rust `easydict_packager` commands.
  - The `rs/README.md` Rust portable checks now point at `pack-rs-portable`, `Package-Portable.ps1`, and `validate-rs-portable` only. Generic `zip-directory` and `build-rust-helpers` commands moved to a standalone diagnostics section that explicitly says they are not the first rs portable assembly path.
  - `easydict_packager --help` now labels `zip-directory` as a generic diagnostics / legacy-hybrid ZIP helper that is not used by rs portable, matching the README split and keeping callers on `pack-rs-portable` for staging plus validation.
  - Renamed default LongDoc/OCR tests from packaged-app-dir wording to neutral `with_app_dir` wording, while keeping retained-worker compatibility tests explicitly named under their feature-gated helper.
- 2026-06-08: Hardened the default rs portable no-runtime boundary after closing MDD support.
  - Re-ran the focused MDD suites and confirmed Rust MDD support is complete for the current core route: raw/base64 resource lookup, cross-record-block payload reads, companion MDD discovery, WebView-ready data-URL inlining, and Quick Translate `rawHtml` separation are covered.
  - No new third-party crate was added. The MDD path continues to use the existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork; the hardening work here stayed on rs packaging/route boundaries rather than changing parser dependencies.
  - `build_rust_helpers(...)` now runs its child `cargo build` with the same `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` environment used by `pack-rs-portable`, so helper-only packaging shims cannot inherit a caller's hybrid profile.
  - Retained-worker runtime profile parsing now treats only the literal `hybrid` value as compatibility opt-in. Old `dotnet` / `dotnet-hybrid` spellings and unknown values remain Rust-only even when the `retained-dotnet-workers` feature is compiled.
  - Added regressions for `validate-rs-portable` rejecting ZIP path traversal entries, Quick CLI explicit OpenVINO grammar failing locally without worker/CompatHost lookup, and LongDoc CLI target Auto failing before provider or retained-worker lookup.
- 2026-06-08: Marked `.NET` runtime extraction as hybrid-only in the Rust packager CLI.
  - No library research was needed for this slice because it does not replace extraction behavior; it exposes the already-enforced runtime-profile guard more clearly to users of the Rust packager.
  - `easydict_packager` usage now shows `extract-dotnet-runtime` with required `--runtime-profile hybrid` and labels it as hybrid/coexistence packaging only, never used by rs portable.
  - Moved the `extract-dotnet-runtime` command out of the `rs/README.md` Rust portable command block and into a separate hybrid-only retained runtime section, so the normal rs portable instructions no longer list a `.NET` runtime extraction command.
  - Updated root `README.md` and `README_ZH.md` so the recommended portable download points at `easydict-rs-portable-*` / `Easydict.Rust.exe`, explicitly says it does not include the `.NET` runtime, and moves the old `easydict_win32-*` / `Easydict.WinUI.exe` package into a legacy/hybrid note.
  - Removed `RustOnly` / `rust-only` from the PowerShell `ValidateSet` parameter completion surface of the legacy dotnet publish/MSIX scripts; they still detect rust-only values internally and point callers to `rs/scripts/Package-Portable.ps1`.
  - Added CLI-unit regressions so future help text cannot reintroduce `[--runtime-profile hybrid|rust-only]` wording, the rs README portable section cannot list `.NET` runtime extraction, the root README recommended portable sections cannot drift back to the legacy .NET package, and dotnet/hybrid scripts cannot advertise rust-only in `RuntimeProfile` completion.
- 2026-06-08: Upgraded the Rust Foundry Local runtime fallback to prepare-aware routing.
  - Reused the existing `lib/easydict-foundry-local` control-plane wrapper; no new dependency was needed. A subagent re-audit also confirmed MDD Rust support is already core-complete, so this slice continued with LocalAI/Foundry because it more directly reduces retained `.NET` worker reliance.
  - Auto Foundry fallback for Quick Translate, streaming Quick Translate, CLI quick translate, and native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF LongDoc now calls `prepare_foundry_local_service(...)`, so the Rust path can run the existing CLI status/start/load lifecycle and then route through the native OpenAI-compatible backend. It no longer depends on Foundry already running before Rust can use it.
  - Explicit FoundryLocal requests with an empty endpoint now use the same prepare lifecycle inside `resolve_openai_compatible_config_for_service(...)`; the resolved endpoint/model are written into the cloned request/config before native dispatch, and non-ready outcomes remain local `ServiceUnavailable` errors rather than retained-worker fallbacks.
  - Added/updated behavior coverage for prepare-aware Auto Foundry route decisions, explicit native Foundry endpoint discovery, CLI Foundry fallback, LongDoc Foundry fallback before worker policy, and OpenAI-compatible config resolution.
- 2026-06-08: Closed the low-level retained-worker packaged-spawn bypass after confirming MDD core support.
  - No new library was needed. Two subagent audits agreed that Rust MDD support is already core-complete on the current route: companion `.mdd` discovery, raw resource lookup, MIME inference, WebView-ready data-URL inlining, failed-resource skipping, and Quick Translate rich HTML separation are covered by `lib/rs-mdict` plus `mdx_native`.
  - `compat_client` now tags packaged LongDoc/LocalAI worker commands with a private retained-worker marker and checks `RetainedWorkerPolicy::from_environment()` inside `WorkerClient::spawn(...)` before any executable I/O probe. Direct callers of `packaged_worker_command(...).spawn()` and `DirectWorkerFacade::spawn_packaged_*` can no longer bypass the higher-level Rust-only helper policy.
  - Added feature-gated `compat_client` regressions for unset, `rust-only`, and explicit `hybrid` runtime profiles, plus a LongDoc explicit-policy regression that keeps injected `RetainedWorkerPolicy::all_enabled()` disabled until the runtime profile is literally `hybrid`.
- 2026-06-08: Hardened LongDoc native output writes and the default process-spawn boundary.
  - Rechecked the MDX/MDD parser options before continuing: `rs-mdict` remains the active permissive route because it is MIT and already supports MDX/MDD, `mdict-rs` is newer and library-first but AGPL-3.0-only, and `mdict-parser` still documents MDD as unimplemented. No parser dependency changed.
  - Native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF LongDoc now prechecks every output target before writing in `Bilingual`/`Both` modes. If the bilingual sidecar path is a directory or its parent cannot be created, the run fails locally before leaving a partial monolingual TXT/MD/PDF output behind.
  - Added regression coverage for text `Both` output and PDF `Both` output where the bilingual target already exists as a directory; both now prove the monolingual output path is untouched after the failure.
  - A parallel subagent added a default rs process-spawn surface guard in `default_api_boundary_behavior`: the default source scan rejects retained `.NET` runtime/worker markers such as `CompatHost`, `Easydict.Workers`, `dotnet.exe`, `DOTNET_ROOT`, and `host/fxr` / `hostfxr`, while keeping retained worker spawn tests and modules feature-gated.
- 2026-06-08: Moved LongDoc native output validation ahead of provider translation.
  - No new library was needed. This closes a native-runner ordering gap in the existing Rust LongDoc export path.
  - `run_native_text_long_document_request_inner(...)` now preflights the Text/Markdown/PDF output targets immediately after source chunk extraction and before document-context prompts or chunk translation. Invalid monolingual or bilingual output paths now fail locally before any native provider/translator call can start.
  - Strengthened the text `Both` and PDF `Both` output-path regressions so they assert the injected translator is not called when the bilingual target is already a directory.
- 2026-06-08: Tightened default LocalDictionary and settings migration Rust-only evidence.
  - No new library was needed. The existing Rust `local_dictionary_index` / `lex_index` route already owns suggestion indexing, and settings migration/storage are pure serde/fs code rather than process-spawn work.
  - Added a default-feature Quick Translate regression proving the native persistent MDX suggestion index stops after the first dictionary fills the 20-item suggestion list. The runner does not open or build indexes for later dictionaries once the result cap is reached, matching the "do not probe later unmigrated dictionaries after native results are full" migration rule.
  - Extended `default_api_boundary_behavior` to scan `settings_migration.rs` and `settings_storage.rs` for retained runtime/worker markers, so the default app/settings path cannot quietly grow a `settings_migrate` CompatHost or worker fallback.
  - A read-only subagent ranked the next slices: rs portable/validator consistency remains the highest release risk, with settings migration and LocalDictionary default-native evidence as low-risk parallel hardening paths.
- 2026-06-08: Locked LocalAI provider aliases and hybrid-only release orchestration.
  - A read-only subagent identified duplicated LocalAI provider alias tables across Quick Translate, LongDoc, state/settings, CLI env overrides, and OpenAI-compatible Foundry config. No new dependency was needed; this is pure protocol/state normalization.
  - Added `protocol_core::normalize_local_ai_provider_mode(...)` and routed Quick Translate, LongDoc, state snapshots, `easydict_cli`, `easydict_long_doc`, and Foundry config checks through it. The shared table now accepts `windows_ai` / `windows-ai` / `phi_silica`, `foundry_local` / `foundry-local` / `local-ai`, and `open_vino` / `open-vino` aliases while keeping unknown values as `Auto`.
  - Added protocol/Quick/LongDoc/CLI regressions proving `open_vino` uses the OpenVINO native preflight path instead of falling back through Auto Foundry or retained worker wording.
  - A separate read-only subagent found release orchestration still used `!= rust-only` gates. Updated release workflows, arm64 smoke, dotnet Makefile, and legacy PowerShell publish/MSIX scripts so retained workers/runtime are published only after an explicit normalized `hybrid` profile; scripts no longer default `RuntimeProfile` to `Hybrid`.
  - Added `easydict_packager` release contract coverage for rs portable rust-only envs, positive retained-worker workflow gates, explicit hybrid PowerShell scripts, and Makefile hybrid-only retained worker/runtime branches.
- 2026-06-08: Extended the default no-runtime API boundary to rs portable binary entrypoints.
  - No new library was needed. This is release-surface hardening after the Rust-native CLI/LongDoc/native-bridge/browser-registrar/lex-index entrypoints already existed.
  - `default_api_boundary_behavior` now scans all default app bin sources (`easydict_cli`, `easydict_long_doc`, `easydict_native_bridge`, `easydict_browser_registrar`, and `easydict_lex_index`) for retained `.NET` runtime/worker markers alongside the library modules. This closes a coverage gap where default APIs were guarded but packaged CLI entrypoints were not explicitly part of the source scan.
- 2026-06-08: Guarded the Store listings workflow against PowerShell YAML parser regressions.
  - No new dependency was needed. `easydict_store_listings` already owns listing YAML parsing with its Rust `serde-saphyr` path, plus validate/preview/summary rendering and msstore payload generation.
  - Filled a submit-payload parity gap by preserving `sortTitle`, `voiceTitle`, and `additionalLicenseTerms` alongside the existing title, short title, copyright, developed-by, and release-notes fields.
  - Added a Rust contract test that includes `.github/workflows/store-listings.yml` and `.winstore/scripts/Sync-StoreListings.ps1`, rejecting `powershell-yaml`, `ConvertFrom-Yaml`, and similar parser re-entry while proving both entrypoints still delegate to `easydict_store_listings`.
  - The same test keeps `MSStore.CLI` installation behind the submit-only workflow branch, so validate/preview/summary stay free of `.NET` tool installation.
- 2026-06-09: Persisted Rust settings cleanup for retired worker isolation keys and tightened Browser NativeBridge event evidence.
  - No new library was needed. This is a settings/storage and NativeBridge contract hardening slice over existing Rust modules.
  - `settings_migration` now keeps the retired runtime-only worker isolation keys in one list and removes `UseLongDocWorker`, `UseLocalAiWorker`, and `UseOcrWorker` regardless of stored value type.
  - `load_settings_file(...)` now persists the migrated JSON back to the same settings file when local migration changes it, so stale retained-worker keys and legacy LocalAI service ids do not remain on disk after normal rs settings load.
  - Added storage/migration regressions proving settings save never writes those keys and file load persists cleanup while preserving the `openvino-local-ai` -> `windows-local-ai` / `LocalAIProvider=OpenVINO` migration.
  - Strengthened `native_bridge_behavior` so the Rust bridge binary is locked to `OCR_TRANSLATE_EVENT_NAME == Local\EasydictRs-OcrTranslate` rather than the dotnet legacy event, and invalid length / incomplete Native Messaging frames produce no response and no OCR signal. The invalid/missing JSON compatibility fallback remains intentionally unchanged.
- 2026-06-09: Extended the default app entrypoint boundary beyond direct `.NET` runtime markers.
  - No new library was needed. This is a default-surface guard over existing Rust app entrypoints and source scans.
  - `default_api_boundary_behavior` now includes `activation.rs` and rejects PowerShell / `.ps1` / `pwsh` / `Compress-Archive`, `dotnet run` / `dotnet publish`, and old `Easydict.NativeBridge` / `Easydict.BrowserRegistrar` project markers on the default rs surface. This prevents rs portable activation/browser/CLI code from quietly reintroducing legacy script or .NET helper entrypoints even when it avoids direct `dotnet.exe` strings.
- 2026-06-09: Guarded release orchestration against retired `.NET` helper project regressions.
  - No new library was needed. This is release-contract hardening around the existing Rust `easydict_packager` helper build path.
  - Added `release_orchestration_uses_rust_helpers_not_retired_dotnet_helper_projects`, which proves `Build-RustHelpers.ps1` delegates to `cargo -p easydict_packager build-rust-helpers`.
  - The same packager contract scans the release workflow, arm64 MSIX smoke workflow, dotnet Makefile, legacy publish/MSIX scripts, and rs portable shim so they cannot reintroduce retired `Easydict.NativeBridge`, `Easydict.BrowserRegistrar`, or `Easydict.CompatHost` `.NET` project references.
  - Mirrored the same source-contract guard in `.NET` `WorkerPackagingTests`, so the existing WinUI configuration test lane also rejects retired helper project references without making the rs package depend on those projects.
- 2026-06-09: Kept hybrid packaging/tooling crates out of the default rs app dependency graph.
  - No new library was needed. This is a manifest-level runtime boundary guard rather than a replacement dependency.
  - Extended `default_api_boundary_behavior` so `easydict_app` and `easydict_preview_iced` cannot depend on hybrid/tooling crates such as `easydict_packager`, `easydict_msix_validate`, Store listing tooling, icon generation, or UI parity analysis.
  - The same test rejects manifest mentions of hybrid runtime/packaging markers such as `extract-dotnet-runtime`, `Package-Msix`, `Build-RustHelpers`, and `Compress-Archive`, keeping `.NET` runtime extraction and release-only tooling out of the first rs portable app binary.
- 2026-06-09: Retired the root LongDoc script's explicit `.NET` debug path.
  - No new library was needed. The script already has a Rust helper and Cargo development mode; this slice removes the remaining opt-in WinUI `dotnet run` fallback.
  - `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1 -UseDotnetLegacy` now fails locally with a retirement message and points callers at `-UseCargo`, `-RustHelperPath`, or `-AppDir` for the Rust `easydict_long_doc.exe` route.
  - Removed the script's legacy argument builder and `dotnet` launcher, and added a packager release-contract regression that rejects `Invoke-DotnetLegacy`, `Easydict.WinUI.csproj`, `dotnet\src\Easydict.WinUI`, and `--translate-long-doc` re-entry.
- 2026-06-09: Hardened MSIX and bundle payload validation against unsafe archive paths.
  - No new dependency was needed. The Rust validator reuses the existing `zip` crate entry metadata (`enclosed_name`) plus explicit component checks for leading roots, `..`, and Windows drive prefixes.
  - `archive_payload_index(...)` now rejects unsafe MSIX entry paths before retained worker / `.NET` runtime payload allowlist checks, so `../workers/...`, `/workers/...`, and `C:/workers/...` cannot bypass Rust-only validation by normalizing differently.
  - `verify_bundle_min_version(...)` now rejects unsafe bundle payload paths and unsafe entries inside nested appx/msix packages. It also accepts an optional runtime profile, and release workflow bundle validation passes `${{ env.RUNTIME_PROFILE }}` so nested packages reuse the same Rust-only/hybrid payload layout policy.
  - Tightened the explicit `hybrid` profile from "required files exist" to a scoped allowlist for the current retained-worker layout. x64/arm64 coexistence packages may keep `workers/longdoc`, `workers/localai`, `dotnet/host/fxr`, and `dotnet/shared/Microsoft.NETCore.App`; `workers/shared` is file-allowlisted to the deduped WinUI/WinRT projection DLLs. Stale roots such as `workers/legacy`, `dotnet/sdk`, `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App`, and `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` now fail validation. Runtime engines or nested runtime layouts inside worker directories, including `coreclr.dll`, `hostfxr.dll`, `System.Private.CoreLib.dll`, `workers/*/host/fxr`, and `workers/*/shared/Microsoft.NETCore.App`, now fail while framework-dependent worker `.runtimeconfig.json` / `.deps.json` metadata remains allowed.
  - `BundleMinVersionOptions::default()` now uses `Some(PackageRuntimeProfile::RustOnly)`, so direct library calls and CLI invocations that omit `--runtime-profile` validate nested appx/msix payload layout with the first rs portable no-runtime policy instead of only checking MinVersion.
  - `create-bundle` now defines its own `RUNTIME_PROFILE` before calling `verify-bundle-minversion --runtime-profile`, so nested package layout validation does not depend on another job's environment.
  - Added regressions for rust-only MSIX unsafe entries, unsafe bundle package paths, unsafe nested package entries, rust-only nested retained runtime payloads, hybrid nested package runtime payload requirements, hybrid app/worker metadata allowance, hybrid worker runtime-engine/stale-root rejection, Rust-only bundle defaults, and the bundle job runtime-profile handoff.
- 2026-06-09: Removed the legacy browser registrar alias from first rs portable packages.
  - No third-party library was needed. This is a package-layout cleanup that separates the Rust portable payload from the retained dotnet/hybrid compatibility helper name.
  - `pack-rs-portable` now stages only `Easydict.Rust.exe`, the Rust-native helper executables, and `README-portable.txt`. It no longer copies `easydict_browser_registrar.exe` to `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` for the first rs portable ZIP.
  - `validate-rs-portable` now treats `BrowserHostRegistrar.exe` as an unexpected first-release portable entry. The later 2026-06-13 helper-build cleanup made that legacy alias an explicit dotnet/hybrid opt-in rather than a generic helper-build side effect.
  - Updated the rs README and migration checklist to keep the alias out of the first portable allowlist while preserving it as an explicit hybrid compatibility artifact.
- 2026-06-09: Neutralized the default rs runtime policy module name.
  - No third-party library was needed. The default app still needs a small local policy object to short-circuit routes that require Rust-native LocalAI/LongDoc support.
  - Moved the default policy implementation from `retained_workers.rs` to `runtime_policy.rs` and renamed the default type to `RuntimeRoutePolicy`. Quick Translate and LongDoc now call `RuntimeRoutePolicy::all_disabled()` in default routes, so the first rs portable compile surface no longer depends on a default `retained_workers` module.
  - `RetainedWorkerPolicy`, `LOCAL_AI_WORKER_DISABLED_MESSAGE`, and `LONGDOC_WORKER_DISABLED_MESSAGE` remain as `retained-dotnet-workers` feature-only compatibility aliases for `compat_client` and retained-worker audit tests.
  - `default_api_boundary_behavior` now scans `runtime_policy.rs` and asserts the crate root does not reintroduce `mod retained_workers;`, while preserving the existing feature-gated crate-root export checks for retained compatibility APIs.
- 2026-06-09: Locked down the default rs process-spawn allowlist.
  - No third-party library was needed. The only default external process boundary should remain the Rust-native Foundry Local CLI wrapper in `lib/easydict-foundry-local`.
  - Added a dynamic `default_api_boundary_behavior` scan over `easydict_app/src/**/*.rs` that rejects direct `std::process::Command`, `Command::new(...)`, and `WorkerCommand::new(...)` usage on the default app surface. `compat_client` and `compat_protocol` are allowed only because the crate root keeps them behind `retained-dotnet-workers`.
  - The same regression asserts the Foundry Local wrapper only spawns `Command::new(&self.executable_name)`, keeps the `EASYDICT_FOUNDRY_LOCAL_CLI` override explicit, and still rejects retained `.NET` runtime/worker markers in that library.
- 2026-06-09: Guarded the Foundry Local CLI override against retained runtime commands.
  - No third-party library was needed; `lib/easydict-foundry-local` already owns the Foundry Local boundary and keeps the optional official `foundry-local-sdk` feature separate from the default CLI route.
  - `CommandFoundryLocalEndpointResolver` now validates the configured executable name before any spawn. Blank values fall back to `foundry`, while `dotnet.exe`, PowerShell/`.ps1`, `hostfxr.dll`, `Easydict.CompatHost*`, `Easydict.Workers.*`, `.runtimeconfig.json`, and bundled `host/fxr` style paths fail locally with a native-CLI-required error.
  - Added Foundry lib regressions for accepted native CLI names and rejected retained runtime/worker/script overrides, tightened the default API boundary test so these forbidden strings are allowed only inside the Foundry CLI denylist, and added Quick/LongDoc CLI regressions proving a fake `Easydict.Workers.LocalAi.exe.cmd` override is not spawned.
- 2026-06-09: Added release-job isolation coverage for first rs portable assets.
  - No new library was needed. This is GitHub Actions release-policy hardening on the existing Rust `easydict_packager` path.
  - Added `rs_portable_release_jobs_stay_isolated_from_dotnet_artifacts`, which section-scans `publish-rs-portable` and `create-rs-portable-release` instead of only checking whole-workflow markers.
  - The test proves the package job runs from the Rust workspace, calls `Package-Portable.ps1`, validates with `validate-rs-portable`, and uses the separate `easydict-rs-portable-*` artifact name.
  - The release job must depend only on `prepare` and `publish-rs-portable`, download only rs portable artifacts, and upload only `rs-portable/*.zip` plus matching `rs-portable/checksums-*.sha256`; both jobs reject dotnet setup, WinApp/MSIX, installer, `.NET` runtime extraction, retained worker, CompatHost, and WinGet markers.
- 2026-06-09: Made the default rs runtime-marker boundary dynamic.
  - No new library was needed. This is a source-contract hardening step over the existing default app boundary test.
  - A read-only subagent found no current default rs path that triggers `.NET` runtime, retained workers, CompatHost, `dotnet.exe`, or PowerShell helpers.
  - `default_process_spawn_surface_has_no_retained_dotnet_runtime_entries` now walks `easydict_app/src/**/*.rs` instead of a fixed file list, skips only feature-gated retained worker modules, and rejects retained runtime/helper markers on every default production source line.
  - This closes the regression gap where a newly added default module could avoid the older hand-maintained marker list while still reintroducing `CompatHost`, `Easydict.Workers`, `dotnet.exe`, `DOTNET_ROOT`, `host/fxr`, PowerShell, or retired helper project markers.
- 2026-06-09: Closed the feature-on Linguee Quick Translate native-route evidence gap.
  - No new library was needed. The existing Rust `traditional_http` module already owns the Linguee request plan and response parser.
  - A read-only subagent ranked Linguee as the smallest core Quick Translate closure item because the HTTP plan/parser existed but the service catalog remains behind `enable-linguee-service`.
  - Added `feature_enabled_linguee_catalog_entry_routes_to_native_traditional_http`, which starts from `find_translation_service_descriptor("linguee")`, proves the request is native-routable, and executes the native traditional HTTP backend with a recording client.
  - The test checks the Linguee service id/name, parsed translation result, and GET request plan against the legacy proxy endpoint and `src`/`dst` language codes, without enabling retained workers or CompatHost.
- 2026-06-09: Added cooperative cancellation coverage to the native LongDoc runner.
  - No new library was needed. This is parity hardening inside the existing Rust-native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF LongDoc batching path.
  - Added a hidden injected-cancellation native runner used by behavior tests while keeping the normal production runner as an always-not-cancelled wrapper.
  - The native runner now checks cancellation before context extraction, before and during chunk scheduling, before each batch translation, and before export.
  - Added a regression proving cancellation after the first chunk stops later chunk scheduling, does not emit the `Exporting` stage, and leaves no partial output file behind.
- 2026-06-09: Closed the Foundry Local SDK adapter boundary inside `lib/easydict-foundry-local`.
  - Rechecked the locked local `foundry-local-sdk 1.2.1` API and the existing lib feature split. No app-crate SDK wrapper was added.
  - `FoundryLocalSdkProvider` now implements the lib-owned `FoundryLocalRuntimeController` / `FoundryLocalEndpointResolver` traits as well as `FoundryLocalSdkModelProvider`, so the existing Foundry prepare/status flow can be reused by an SDK-backed runtime without exposing SDK types in `easydict_app`.
  - Added feature-gated compile coverage proving the real SDK provider satisfies both trait boundaries, and default app boundary coverage proving the app manifest does not enable `sdk` / `sdk-winml` and app source does not reference `foundry_local_sdk` / `FoundryLocalSdkProvider`.
- 2026-06-09: Closed the default Bing Quick Translate catalog-to-native evidence gap.
  - No new library was needed. The Rust-native two-phase Bing backend and request parser already exist.
  - Added a regression that starts from `find_translation_service_descriptor("bing")`, proves the default catalog entry is native-routable, and runs the native Bing backend with a recording two-phase client.
  - The test checks the default `www.bing.com` host, `ttranslatev3` request plan, translated text, service id/name, and detected source language without retained worker or CompatHost involvement.
  - Added a Bing-specific unsupported-language regression proving invalid language pairs fail locally before fetching the translator session page or building a translate request.
- 2026-06-09: Added native LongDoc OpenVINO/NLLB success-path evidence.
  - Reused the existing `lib/easydict-nllb` tokenizer/engine traits and `NativeOpenVinoQuickTranslateBackend`; no new inference dependency was added.
  - Added an injected LongDoc translator test that runs a `windows-local-ai` / OpenVINO text document request through a fake NLLB tokenizer/engine, verifies quick-translate request mapping, forced BOS token usage, and translated output file contents.
  - This closes the evidence gap between the already-tested Quick Translate OpenVINO backend and LongDoc's native chunk runner.
- 2026-06-09: Added runtime env execution evidence for `build-rust-helpers`.
  - Evaluated `assert_cmd`/`assert_fs` as possible CLI-test helpers, but kept this slice on `std` because a tiny fake `cargo`/`rustup` executable gives a narrower contract test without adding dependencies.
  - Added `build_rust_helpers_child_cargo_is_forced_to_rust_only_runtime_profile`, which runs `build_rust_helpers` with fake child tooling and proves inherited `hybrid` profile envs are overridden to `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` before helper builds.
  - The same test verifies the helper build command line and copied helper outputs, tightening the first rs portable no-runtime boundary.
- 2026-06-09: Hardened the native Bing session parser against translator-page drift.
  - Rechecked reusable HTML parser options first: `scraper`/`html5ever` are useful for DOM/selector parsing, and `lol_html` is strong for streaming rewrite, but Bing's fragile fields are mostly JavaScript values inside script text. No new dependency was added; the existing local scanner was a better fit for JS string literal handling and default rs no-worker behavior.
  - `parse_bing_credentials_from_html` now accepts bare or quoted `IG` properties with single/double quoted values and JS escapes, whitespace/single-quote/unquoted `data-iid` attributes, and single-quote/escaped `params_AbusePreventionHelper` tokens.
  - The abuse-prevention parser now scans all marker occurrences, skips identifier-boundary noise and malformed arrays, and accepts boot-data property arrays such as `"params_AbusePreventionHelper":[key,"token",expiry]`.
  - Added `traditional_http_behavior` regressions for minified/single-quote/escaped script values, noise before valid script fields, invalid marker candidates before a valid array, and boot-data property credentials.
- 2026-06-09: Added real multi-MDD first-hit evidence for native HTML inlining.
  - Reused the existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork and test fixture writer; no new parser dependency was needed.
  - Added a native MDX lookup test with a mock MDX definition and two real minimal `.mdd` files containing the same image key. The result proves app-level HTML inlining goes through `RsMdictMddReaderFactory` and uses the first MDD hit instead of a later duplicate.
- 2026-06-09: Reconfirmed MDD core support and replaced remaining small hand-written base64 helpers.
  - Re-ran the focused MDD suites after the user-requested MDD check: `lib/rs-mdict` MDD tests, app `mdx_native_behavior mdd`, and Quick Translate `mdd` all pass. The current Rust route covers MDD parsing, raw resource lookup, cross-record-block reads, companion discovery, WebView-ready data-URL inlining, and first-hit behavior across multiple MDD files.
  - Reused the existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` fork for MDD and the already-present `base64` crate for credential/OCR payload encoding; no new dependency was added. For credential decode, the `base64` crate engine is configured to require standard padding while allowing historical trailing bits that the old hand-written decoder accepted.
  - Added credential tests for malformed payloads, missing padding rejection, and legacy trailing-bit compatibility; tightened OCR tests to decode BMP/JPEG data URL payloads with the standard padded engine and assert concrete image markers.
- 2026-06-09: Filled the remaining core MDD gaps found by the focused audit.
  - Settings load now auto-discovers companion `.mdd` / numbered `.MDD` files for imported MDX dictionaries whose legacy settings did not persist `MddFilePaths`, matching the .NET-era behavior without touching UI code.
  - Native MDX lookup now returns an `mddResourcesInlined` signal only when MDD resource rewriting actually changed entry HTML. Quick Translate uses that signal instead of merely checking whether the settings contain MDD paths, so missing/unopenable MDD files no longer cause misleading `rawHtml` retention.
  - Added regression coverage for legacy settings MDD discovery, all-MDD-open-failure no-inline signaling, and Quick Translate dropping rich HTML when no MDD resource was actually inlined.
- 2026-06-09: Strengthened default MDD fixture coverage for compressed/non-ASCII resources.
  - No new parser dependency was added. The test reuses `flate2`, which `lib/rs-mdict` already uses for MDict zlib block handling, and keeps the existing MIT `lib/rs-mdict` route.
  - Added a generated MDD fixture with a zlib-compressed record block, non-ASCII image/audio/CSS resource keys, and larger binary payloads. The test verifies raw lookup, prefix lookup, MIME metadata, and standard-base64 `locate(...)` output.
  - This does not replace real-corpus testing, but it closes a default-test blind spot that previously required local `RS_MDICT_TEST_MDD` fixtures to exercise compressed resource blocks and Unicode resource names.
- 2026-06-09: Replaced the remaining hand-written `lib/rs-mdict` RIPEMD-128 implementation with RustCrypto.
  - Rechecked the reusable hash implementation first: RustCrypto `ripemd` is a pure-Rust RIPEMD crate, exposes `Ripemd128`, documents the crate as MIT OR Apache-2.0 licensed, and already fits the RustCrypto stack used elsewhere in the app (`https://docs.rs/ripemd/latest/ripemd/`).
  - `lib/rs-mdict` now depends on `ripemd = "0.2"` and keeps the old public `ripemd128(&[u8]) -> [u8; 16]` compatibility wrapper, so MDX/MDD decryption callers do not change while the local hash code is no longer maintained by hand.
  - Retained the existing RIPEMD-128 vector tests and re-ran encrypted MDX behavior tests that exercise credential-derived dictionary lookup.
- 2026-06-11: Made legacy Quick CLI host/app-dir hints unconditional no-op compatibility inputs.
  - No library research was needed. This is default rs runtime-surface cleanup after the retained LocalAI worker route had already been removed from the default CLI.
  - `easydict_cli` no longer rejects command lines that pass both legacy `--host` and `--app-dir`; both values, plus `--host-arg`, are parsed only to keep old scripts from failing and are ignored before route selection.
  - Added CLI behavior coverage proving a `windows-local-ai` request with both legacy hints still fails locally with Rust-native-route wording in a hybrid environment and does not probe retained LocalAI worker paths or mention CompatHost.
- 2026-06-11: Added fake-cargo execution evidence for the first rs portable build path.
  - No new dependency was needed; this extends the existing std-based fake `cargo`/`rustup` release-contract test harness.
  - Added `pack_rs_portable_child_cargo_is_forced_to_rust_only_runtime_profile`, which runs `pack_rs_portable(...)` with parent env set to `hybrid`, records the preview-app and helper child cargo invocations, and proves both receive `EASYDICT_RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only` / `RUNTIME_PROFILE=rust-only`.
  - The same regression asserts the child cargo args do not enable `retained-dotnet-workers`, `--features`, or `--all-features`, and that the staged first-release payload contains only the six allowed Rust portable entries.
- 2026-06-11: Added Rust-only boundary tests to the rs portable release job.
  - No new dependency was needed. This strengthens release orchestration by running existing Rust contract tests before the portable ZIP is built.
  - `publish-rs-portable` now runs `easydict_packager --test release_contract_behavior`, `easydict_app --test default_api_boundary_behavior`, and default `protocol_behavior` before `Package-Portable.ps1`.
  - Extended `release_contract_behavior` so the release workflow cannot drop those pre-package Rust-only boundary tests while still keeping the job isolated from setup-dotnet, MSIX, installer, WinGet, retained worker, and CompatHost markers.
- 2026-06-11: Removed the silent Hybrid default from dotnet runtime-profile entry points.
  - No library research was needed; this is release/runtime policy cleanup around the existing Rust packager and MSBuild profile gates.
  - `dotnet/Makefile` no longer defaults `RUNTIME_PROFILE` to `hybrid`. Legacy dotnet portable/MSIX paths must opt in with `RUNTIME_PROFILE=hybrid`, while ordinary build/test commands no longer silently select a retained-worker/runtime-producing profile.
  - `Easydict.WinUI.csproj` now treats an omitted `RuntimeProfile` as `RustOnly`, which disables retained worker ProjectReferences/copy and the in-proc LongDoc fallback by default. Explicit `RuntimeProfile=Hybrid` still preserves the coexistence path.
  - Rust `release_contract_behavior` and `.NET` `WorkerPackagingTests` text guards were updated so future changes cannot reintroduce `RUNTIME_PROFILE ?= hybrid` or a csproj empty-profile Hybrid default.
  - MSBuild property checks now show the empty profile as `RuntimeProfile=RustOnly`, `BuildWorkerOutputs=false`, and `EnableInProcLongDocFallback=false`; explicit `RuntimeProfile=Hybrid` still reports `RuntimeProfile=Hybrid` and `EnableInProcLongDocFallback=true`.
  - Attempted the targeted `.NET` `WorkerPackagingTests` run twice after waiting, but the test project currently fails before executing those tests because unrelated LongDoc/layout test sources reference stale symbols such as `DocLayoutYoloService`, `LayoutDetectionStrategy`, and ambiguous `DocumentOutputMode`. Those files were left untouched.
- 2026-06-11: Reconfirmed the first rs portable payload and native PDF fallback boundary.
  - No new dependency was added. The LongDoc PDF path keeps the existing Rust-native stack: `pdfium-render` / `lib/easydict-pdf-render` as an optional source-extraction first pass, then `pdf-extract`, `lopdf` content-stream fallback, and OCR fallback.
  - A read-only subagent audit found no default rs path that starts `.NET` runtime, retained workers, CompatHost, `dotnet.exe`, or PowerShell helpers. An actual Debug no-zip `Package-Portable.ps1` smoke produced the six allowed first-release files and passed `validate-rs-portable`, with no `dotnet/`, `workers/`, runtimeconfig, or `.NET` DLL payload.
  - Added a private `read_native_pdf_source_chunks_with_fallbacks(...)` helper and regression proving a PDF source-extractor failure recovers through native text fallback before OCR and without any worker route.
  - Re-ran focused LongDoc PDF and native text runner behavior tests after the helper split; the existing output precheck regressions still prove `Both`/`Bilingual` modes fail before writing partial TXT/MD/PDF sidecar outputs.
- 2026-06-11: Locked the legacy Inno installer behind the explicit Hybrid profile.
  - No new dependency was needed. A read-only packaging subagent found the core rs portable path already forces Rust-only and validates payloads, but the old `Build-Installer.ps1` entry point still lacked its own runtime-profile gate.
  - `dotnet/scripts/Build-Installer.ps1` now requires `-RuntimeProfile Hybrid` before probing Inno Setup or packaging the legacy dotnet publish output. Missing profiles, `rust-only` aliases, and unknown values fail locally and redirect first-release callers to `rs/scripts/Package-Portable.ps1`.
  - `dotnet/Makefile` now passes `$(RUNTIME_PROFILE)` into all `installer-*` targets, so the publish and installer stages cannot silently diverge on runtime policy.
  - Strengthened the OCR no-worker regression by running the app-dir OCR path against a temp directory containing stale `workers/ocr` and `Easydict.CompatHost.exe` markers; Windows Native OCR still stays on the Rust-native local path and reports no worker/CompatHost wording.
- 2026-06-11: Let Makefile MSIX validation use the Rust validator's Rust-only default.
  - No new dependency was needed. A read-only packaging subagent confirmed the old `validate-msix` recipe passed `--runtime-profile "$(RUNTIME_PROFILE)"` even when the Makefile default was empty, turning the safe Rust-only default into a CLI parse error.
  - `dotnet/Makefile validate-msix` now normalizes `RUNTIME_PROFILE` and passes `--runtime-profile` only when the caller provided a value; with an empty profile it calls `easydict_msix_validate -- "$(MSIX)"`, letting the validator's default `runtime-profile=rust-only` policy reject retained workers/runtime.
  - Extended `release_contract_behavior` so the Makefile target must keep the explicit-profile and omitted-profile branches and cannot reintroduce `--runtime-profile "$(RUNTIME_PROFILE)"`.
- 2026-06-11: Added native LongDoc `resultJsonPath` sidecar parity.
  - No new dependency was needed. The native runner reuses existing `serde_json` and the same output preflight helpers used by TXT/MD/PDF outputs.
  - Native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF LongDoc now prechecks `TranslateDocumentParams.result_json_path` before provider translation starts. A sidecar path that is a directory fails locally before any provider call or partial output write.
  - Successful native runs now write the full `TranslateDocumentResult` JSON to the requested sidecar path and return that path in `resultJsonPath`, closing another retained LongDoc worker parity gap for large/result-file workflows.
- 2026-06-11: Added a LocalAI route-decision matrix for Rust-native-first routing.
  - No new dependency was needed. This is a route-contract regression over the existing Quick Translate decision function.
  - `quick_translate_behavior` now covers Auto/WindowsAI/Foundry/OpenVINO translation, streaming, grammar, cache-ready/cache-missing, and target-auto cases in one table-driven test. The same matrix is also checked under `retained-dotnet-workers` with an explicit hybrid worker policy, proving these native/preflight routes still do not fall through to `RetainedWorkerCompat`.
- 2026-06-11: Wired LongDoc `resultJsonPath` through the Rust CLI and PowerShell shim.
  - No new dependency was needed. The CLI forwards `--result-json` / `--result-json-path` into `TranslateDocumentParams.result_json_path`, and non-JSON CLI output prints the sidecar path when a native run succeeds.
  - `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1` now accepts `-ResultJsonPath` and passes `--result-json` to `easydict_long_doc`, keeping the root script Rust-only while exposing the native result sidecar.
  - Added CLI coverage proving a conflicting sidecar path is prechecked before provider lookup, plus release-contract coverage that the PowerShell shim keeps the sidecar pass-through and still does not launch legacy .NET LongDoc mode.
- 2026-06-11: Made unsupported MDX encryption errors win before credential prompts.
  - Rechecked current Rust MDict options before touching the route: `rs-mdict` still covers the supported MDX/MDD route already in-tree, while the newer Rust MDict crates found during scouting either add replacement risk or carry AGPL licensing. No dependency changed.
  - `Encrypted=3` / unknown encrypted MDX modes are now classified as unsupported before credential validation. Missing regcode/email no longer produces a misleading credentials-required error for modes the Rust-native reader cannot support.
  - Added regressions for native lookup, Quick Translate MDX services, and local dictionary suggestions proving unsupported encryption fails locally before opening the MDX reader and without CompatHost wording.
- 2026-06-11: Persisted native LongDoc checkpoints in result sidecars.
  - No new dependency was needed. The native runner now reuses the existing `long_document_export` and `pdf_export_blocks` checkpoint shapes, adding serde coverage so the same structures can become the later Retry Failed restore input.
  - Native Text/Markdown/simple-PDF result JSON sidecars still flatten the unchanged `TranslateDocumentResult`, but now also include route metadata plus text and optional PDF checkpoints with source chunks, chunk metadata, translated chunks, and failed chunk indexes.
  - Added LongDoc behavior coverage for successful text sidecars, partial-success sidecars with failed chunks absent from `translatedChunks`, and PDF sidecars that remain result-compatible while carrying the PDF checkpoint.
- 2026-06-11: Added native LongDoc Retry Failed backend restore.
  - No new dependency was needed. The backend reads the existing result JSON sidecar, restores text/PDF checkpoints, seeds already translated chunks, disables document-context retry work like the .NET retry path, and only sends failed chunk indexes back through the native translator/cache/formula-protection route.
  - Retry success now merges translated chunks, clears failed indexes, reexports TXT/MD/simple-PDF outputs, and rewrites the sidecar. Continued failures stay `PartiallyCompleted`; a completed sidecar with no failed chunks reexports without provider calls.
  - UI still needs to wire its Retry Failed entrypoint to this backend helper; this step intentionally avoided `ui.rs`, `state.rs`, `i18n.rs`, and UI contract files while another agent is editing them.
- 2026-06-11: Wired LongDoc Retry Failed through the Rust CLI and PowerShell shim.
  - No new dependency was needed. `easydict_long_doc --retry-failed --result-json <file>` now calls the native sidecar restore helper; missing `--result-json` fails locally before settings/provider work.
  - `scripts/translate-long-doc.ps1` now exposes `-RetryFailed`, requires `-ResultJsonPath`, and passes `--retry-failed` to the Rust helper in both packaged-helper and cargo modes while keeping `-UseDotnetLegacy` retired.
  - Added CLI coverage for the retry argument contract and a real no-provider sidecar reexport path, plus release-contract coverage that the shim keeps retry on the Rust side and cannot reintroduce dotnet/CompatHost launch markers.
- 2026-06-11: Locked the LocalDictionary index default root as an intentional `.NET`/Rust cache coexistence point.
  - No new dependency was needed. A read-only subagent rechecked this against the `.NET LocalDictionaryIndexService` and confirmed `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Easydict\mdx_index` is a compatible LXDX/index-manifest cache, not a `.NET runtime`, worker, named-event, registry, or process-launch boundary.
  - Added an inline comment on `default_local_dictionary_index_root()` so the shared root does not get confused with the rs-specific NativeBridge/event roots that must use `EasydictRs`.
  - Added `native_local_dictionary_index_default_root_uses_legacy_cache_for_dotnet_coexistence`, which locks the default root to the legacy shared cache while proving `LocalDictionaryIndexService::new()` does not create an `EasydictRs\mdx_index` folder by default.
- 2026-06-11: Wired Quick Translate Youdao phonetic enrichment into the Rust app task path.
  - No new dependency was needed. The route reuses the already migrated Youdao web dictionary executor plus the existing `PhoneticMemoryCache`, flight tracker, enrichment planner, and merge helpers from `translation_cache`.
  - Non-streaming Quick Translate backend tasks now attempt phonetic enrichment before emitting `QuickTranslateServiceFinished`, so successful provider results can be cached and rendered with merged US/UK phonetics. Streaming, grammar, MDX, Youdao itself, non-English targets, sentence-like outputs, existing target phonetics, and `NoResult` stay untouched.
  - Added focused Quick Translate coverage for Youdao phonetic fetch/merge/cache insert, skip conditions, and phonetic-cache hits that avoid a Youdao HTTP request. Youdao fetch failures remain non-fatal by returning the original translation update.
- 2026-06-13: Decoupled startup activation parsing from WinFluent task types.
  - No new dependency was needed. Startup activation is only CLI/protocol parsing plus internal message selection, so the route now stays in `activation.rs` as pure app-core logic and `lib.rs` wraps the optional message into a WinFluent `Task` at the application boundary.
  - `startup_activation_message_for_args(...)` now covers the OCR activation message mapping without importing `win_fluent`; the existing `startup_activation_task_for_args(...)` API remains available from the crate root for app startup and tests.
  - Added a default boundary guard so `activation.rs` cannot reintroduce `win_fluent`, `Task<`, or `Task::` while the Quick Translate behavior test continues proving startup OCR activation reuses the hotkey capture-overlay path.
- 2026-06-13: Made the packaged Rust GUI entrypoint default to production state instead of preview demo state.
  - No new dependency was needed. `easydict_preview_iced.exe` is still the first-release binary copied to `Easydict.Rust.exe`, but the wrapper now only enters preview harness behavior when an `EASYDICT_PREVIEW_*` environment variable is present.
  - Without preview env, the packaged GUI loads real settings from `default_settings_storage_path()`, uses `main_window_options_for_settings(...)`, clears demo source text/results/LongDoc history/floating-window sample content, and snapshots loaded settings as saved settings.
  - Preview-only affordances remain available for UI automation and screenshots: preview title/placement/sizing, schema dump, auto-toggle/scroll, and pop-button preview state still run when the preview env prefix is present.
- 2026-06-13: Hid `.NET` runtime extraction from the default packager CLI/API surface.
  - No replacement library was needed because this slice only narrows exposure. The existing hybrid-only extraction path still uses the already selected `reqwest` + `zip` implementation behind `hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging`.
  - Default `easydict_packager` builds no longer recognize `extract-dotnet-runtime` as a named command or keep a feature-disabled fallback. The command is now only compiled in explicit hybrid builds.
  - `ExtractDotnetRuntime*`, `PackageRuntimeProfile`, `extract_dotnet_runtime_archive(...)`, `dotnet_runtime_url(...)`, and extraction-only helpers/tests are now gated behind `hybrid-dotnet-runtime-packaging`, while release-contract coverage ensures they do not leak back into the default rs portable API surface.
- 2026-06-14: Revalidated Rust MDX/MDD support with the user's Collins COBUILD English Usage real corpus.
